title: M1: Skeleton & config plane
description: Umbrella for WS-A. Stand up package, entry point, bootstrap event wiring, config loader/validator, logger, and the single computed enabled-set. After this lands, WS-C/D/E/F/G can begin in parallel where dependencies allow. See docs/plans/pi-blackbytes-mvp.md §4 WS-A and §8 Slice 1. Note: deps says 'blocks' meaning M0 must be closed first.
close_reason: All M1 children complete: skeleton, config, handlers, enabled-set, test harness

title: M1.1: Initialize npm package skeleton (package.json, tsconfig, deps, build)
description:
## Objective
Create the baseline npm package skeleton for the extension so later leaves can build against a real package: `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `.gitignore`, and a stub `README.md`, with the Pi metadata fields required by PRD §4.1.

## Context
This is the first concrete WS-A implementation bead. All later source files, tests, and build outputs assume this package contract exists and that `dist/index.js` is the published extension entry.

## Scope
IN:
- `package.json` for `@blackbytes/pi-blackbytes` version `0.1.0`
- `pi.extensions: ["./dist/index.js"]`
- `pi.skills: ["./skills/**/SKILL.md"]`
- strict Node 20+ ESM TypeScript config
- baseline scripts for `build` and `test`
- dependency/peerDependency wiring:
  - dependency: `@sinclair/typebox ^0.33`
  - dependency: `zod ^4`
  - peerDependency: `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent ^0.67`
OUT:
- extension bootstrap code, tools, handlers, or real docs content

## Implementation Context
- Files touched: `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `.gitignore`, `README.md`.
- Build output target must be compatible with a future `src/index.ts` compiled to `dist/index.js`.
- Keep the layout minimal; only add scripts/config needed by immediate downstream beads.

## Constraints
- Runtime target: Node >= 20.
- Package size budget (<500KB gzipped) is verified later in M6, not here.
- Reuse built-in npm/TypeScript tooling; do not introduce extra packaging layers unless required for the build to work.

## DoD
- `npm install` succeeds.
- `npm run build` emits `dist/index.js` from the package skeleton.
- Package metadata matches PRD §4.1 exactly enough for Pi to discover the extension and bundled skills.
close_reason: Implemented: package skeleton complete

title: M1.2: Implement extension entry point and bootstrap scaffold
description:
## Objective
Create the extension entry point and bootstrap scaffold: `src/index.ts` exports `default function (pi: ExtensionAPI): void`, and `src/bootstrap.ts` wires the event subscriptions required by PRD §4.3.

## Context
Pi discovers the extension through the default export. Bootstrap is the single composition point where session lifecycle hooks and future tool registration will be connected. This bead should leave obvious integration seams for later leaves without implementing their real logic yet.

## Scope
IN:
- `src/index.ts` that delegates to `bootstrap(pi)`
- `src/bootstrap.ts` with `pi.on(...)` subscriptions for:
  - `session_start`
  - `resources_discover`
  - `before_agent_start`
  - `model_select`
  - `before_provider_request`
  - `tool_call`
  - `tool_result`
  - `session_shutdown`
- each handler wrapped in a shared safe-execution pattern that logs failures and surfaces `ctx.ui.notify("error", ...)` when context is available
OUT:
- real handler behavior, enabled-set computation, and concrete tool registration

## Implementation Context
- Prefer importing placeholder handler functions from dedicated modules or obvious TODO stubs so later leaves can fill them in without reshaping bootstrap.
- Add a small startup log such as `pi-blackbytes vX.Y loaded` using package version metadata or a local constant.
- This bead should define the integration skeleton, not business logic.

## Constraints
- Handler failures must never crash the extension.
- Do not register tools here yet; that happens after the enabled-set strategy is implemented.
- Keep the scaffold simple enough that later leaves can replace placeholders incrementally.

## DoD
- Pi can load the extension without throwing.
- All required PRD §4.3 subscriptions exist in bootstrap.
- A minimal test or smoke harness proves the default export invokes bootstrap and the scaffold loads cleanly.
close_reason: Implemented: extension entry point (src/index.ts), bootstrap scaffold (src/bootstrap.ts) with all 8 PRD §4.3 event subscriptions wrapped in safe-execution pattern, placeholder handlers (src/handlers/index.ts), types (src/types/pi.ts). 2 tests pass.

title: M1.3: Implement buffered file logger with daily rotation (10MB cap)
description:
## Objective
Port the buffered file logger from oc-blackbytes into `src/shared/logger.ts`. Logs to `~/.pi/logs/pi-blackbytes.log` (fallback `/tmp/pi-blackbytes.log`), buffered writes, daily rotation, 10MB max per file.

## Context
PRD §6 logging requirement. Used by every other workstream for diagnostics. Must redact secrets (Decision 8).

## Scope
IN: logger module with levels (debug/info/warn/error), buffered append, flush on `session_shutdown`, daily rotation, size cap, secret-redaction helper for known fields (`api_key`, `authorization`, `exa_api_key`, `tavily_api_key`, `x-initiator` is fine).
OUT: integration into handlers (later).

## Implementation Context
- Expected files: `src/shared/logger.ts`, any small rotation/redaction helpers, and tests.
- The logger should be reusable from handlers, commands, and nested-runner code without forcing callers to know filesystem details.
- Shutdown flush wiring lands in a later bead, so expose an explicit flush API now.

## Constraints
- NEVER write secret values: known sensitive keys must be redacted to `***`.
- Must not block the event loop on writes.
- Failure to write log MUST NOT throw.

## DoD
- Unit tests: rotation triggers at size, secrets redacted, flush works on shutdown.
close_reason: Implemented: buffered file logger (src/shared/logger.ts) with debug/info/warn/error levels, 4KB buffer with 5s flush interval, daily rotation, 10MB size cap, secret redaction for api_key/authorization/exa_api_key/tavily_api_key. 7 tests pass.

title: M1.4: Implement Zod v4 config schema (BlackbytesConfigSchema)
description:
## Objective
Port the config Zod schema from oc-blackbytes into `src/config/schema.ts`. Schema per PRD §4.7:
- `disabled_tools: string[]` optional
- `disabled_sub_agents: enum array (explore|oracle|librarian|general)` optional
- `hashline_edit: boolean` default true
- `copilot_initiator_header: boolean` default true
- `websearch: { provider: 'exa'|'tavily', exa_api_key, tavily_api_key }` optional
- `context7: { api_key }` optional
- `sub_agents: record<string, { model, reasoningEffort, temperature }>` optional

## Context
The config block lives at `~/.pi/agent/settings.json › blackbytes`. This schema is the contract for `session_start` parsing, enabled-set computation, and every downstream feature that reads Blackbytes config.

## Scope
IN:
- `src/config/schema.ts` with Zod v4
- default values
- exports `BlackbytesConfig` type and `parseBlackbytesConfig(unknown)` returning `{ ok: true; value } | { ok: false; errors }`
- tests for validation behavior and defaults
OUT:
- file IO / settings loading (next leaf)

## Implementation Context
- Reuse the oc-blackbytes schema shape where it matches the approved PRD, but align names/defaults to this repo's canonical config block.
- The parser result should be ergonomic for `session_start`: valid config yields typed data, invalid config yields user-displayable errors without throwing.
- Unknown-key behavior matters because users may keep extra settings in their JSON; preserve them per the contract rather than silently stripping the whole object.

## Constraints
- Use Zod v4.
- Validate only the `blackbytes` subtree, not the whole Pi settings file.
- Unknown keys ignored (not stripped).
- Error output should be clear enough for UI notification / troubleshooting.

## DoD
- Unit tests: empty object validates with defaults; invalid types report clear errors; unknown keys ignored (not stripped).
- Exported type/parser signatures are stable enough for later config-loader leaves to consume directly.
close_reason: Implemented: Zod v4 config schema (src/config/schema.ts) with BlackbytesConfigSchema, BlackbytesConfig type, parseBlackbytesConfig function. All PRD §4.7 fields with defaults, passthrough for unknown keys. 5 tests pass.

title: M1.5: Implement settings loader with safe IO and atomic-friendly read
description:
## Objective
Implement the config loader that reads Pi settings, extracts the `blackbytes` block, validates it, and returns a safe parsed config without mutating unrelated settings.

## Context
This loader is called during `session_start` and feeds the enabled-set computation plus later handler/tool behavior. It must be robust to missing files, malformed JSON, and invalid user config while preserving the rule that handler failures never crash Pi.

## Scope
IN:
- `src/config/loader.ts`
- read path resolution:
  - `process.env.PI_AGENT_DIR/settings.json` when `PI_AGENT_DIR` is set
  - otherwise `~/.pi/agent/settings.json`
- extract only the top-level `blackbytes` object
- validate via `BlackbytesConfigSchema`
- return defaults when the file is missing, malformed, absent `blackbytes`, or contains invalid `blackbytes` data
- log/notify failure modes without leaking secret values
OUT:
- writing settings (`/setup-models`), JSONC support, or mutation of non-`blackbytes` settings

## Implementation Context
- Expected collaborators: schema from `M1.4`, logger from `M1.3`, bootstrap session-start flow from `M1.2`.
- Keep the loader API simple for downstream use, e.g. `loadBlackbytesConfig(): Promise<BlackbytesConfig>` (exact signature can vary).
- Document where defaults live so later beads reuse the same source.

## Constraints
- Parse strict JSON only; no JSONC/comments/trailing-comma support.
- Never log raw secret values or full provider credential objects.
- The loader must return a safe config/defaults result instead of throwing.

## DoD
- Tests cover: missing file, malformed JSON, missing `blackbytes`, invalid `blackbytes`, valid config.
- Failure paths notify/log with redacted details only.
- The returned config is ready for direct use by `session_start` without extra validation.
close_reason: Implemented: settings loader (src/config/loader.ts) reads PI_AGENT_DIR or ~/.pi/agent/settings.json, extracts blackbytes block, validates via schema, returns defaults on any failure. 5 tests pass.

title: M1.6: Compute single enabled-set for tools/sub-agents/skills at session_start
description:
## Objective
At `session_start`, compute the single immutable enabled-set for the session and expose it through a shared accessor used by registration, discovery, prompt/resource exposure, and status surfaces.

## Context
This bead implements Decision 1 after `M0.Q3` confirms the allowed activation strategy. The enabled-set is the anti-drift mechanism: every consumer must agree on the same tools, sub-agents, and skills so the model is never shown capabilities it cannot actually invoke.

## Scope
IN:
- session-start computation flow that reads validated config and derives the enabled set
- shared accessor such as `getEnabledSet(): { tools: Set<string>; subAgents: Set<string>; skills: Set<string> }`
- support for `disabled_tools` (exact public tool names) and `disabled_sub_agents` (delegate persona names)
- immutable per-session behavior; no recomputation in random modules
- implementation strategy chosen by `M0.Q3`:
  - either register/expose only enabled entries
  - or register broadly then narrow through the Pi API, if proven correct
OUT:
- actual per-tool registration logic, `<available_resources>` rendering, and `/blackbytes-status` output formatting

## Implementation Context
- Expected collaborators: config loader (`M1.5`), bootstrap/session_start scaffold (`M1.2`), future registration helper (`M2a.0`), and handler/resource surfaces in WS-E/WS-G.
- Define clearly where session-scoped state lives and how later handlers read it without recomputing.
- If the chosen strategy has Pi API caveats, encode them directly in the accessor/registration flow rather than in comments alone.

## Constraints
- Single source of truth: no duplicated enable/disable logic elsewhere.
- Must follow the exact strategy proven in `M0.Q3`.
- MVP does not support mid-session toggling.

## DoD
- Unit tests prove `disabled_tools` and `disabled_sub_agents` are applied correctly.
- Tests prove the enabled-set is computed once per session and reused.
- Integration or smoke test verifies `session_start` completes within the intended budget (<200ms with realistic config).
close_reason: Implemented enabled-set module

title: M1.7: Add baseline integration test harness (Pi mock + session_start happy/error paths)
description:
## Objective
Stand up the integration test harness used by all later workstreams: a minimal mock of `ExtensionAPI` (`registerTool`, `registerCommand`, `on`, `setActiveTools`, `appendEntry`) so handlers can be exercised in tests. Cover `session_start` with valid config and with malformed settings.

## Context
PRD §6 mandates integration tests for the critical extension surfaces, each with success and error paths. This leaf provides the reusable harness and first pair of session-start tests so later beads can add focused coverage instead of rebuilding the Pi mock each time.

## Scope
IN:
- `test/test-utils/pi-mock.ts`
- two `session_start` tests (success path, malformed-settings path)
- test script entry / wiring so the suite runs in CI
OUT:
- per-handler test matrices for other events (covered by later leaves)

## Implementation Context
- The mock should cover only the ExtensionAPI surface Blackbytes actually uses in MVP.
- Later M3/M4/M6 test beads will extend this harness, so keep helper names and event simulation APIs stable and reusable.
- Success-path config should exercise defaults plus one or two non-default settings to prove parsing/bootstrapping works.

## Constraints
- Keep the mock lightweight; no need to emulate Pi internals unrelated to this repo's handlers.
- Error-path coverage should verify graceful handling of malformed settings rather than uncaught throws.
- Avoid locking tests to incidental log strings unless those strings are part of the contract.

## DoD
- `npm test` runs and passes both `session_start` tests.
- Harness is reusable enough that later handler-test beads can add coverage without replacing the mock.
close_reason: Implemented: Pi mock harness + 2 session_start integration tests

description: Umbrella for WS-E. Implements the handlers that make Bytes feel like Bytes. Depends on bundled+http tools so the resources block lists real tool names. See plan §4 WS-E, §8 Slice 3.

title: M3.1: Hashline rewrite of Pi 'read' tool output (LINE#ID anchors)
description:
## Objective
Subscribe `tool_result` event; when `event.toolName === "read"` and `!event.isError` and `cfg.hashline_edit === true`, rewrite the text content block into `LINE#ID|content` anchored format and return updated content.

## Context
PRD §4.5, plan Decision 5. This is what makes `hashline_edit` precise — the LLM sees stable line anchors. Hashline rewrite scope is NARROW: read tool output only; do NOT touch Pi's `edit` tool output.

## Scope
IN: `src/handlers/tool-result.ts` (read branch), helper to compute deterministic 2-letter CID from set `ZPMQVRWSNKTXJBYH` per line, reuse oc-blackbytes anchor format `{n}#{ID}|{content}`.
OUT: write rewrite (next leaf), edit tool changes (forbidden).

## Implementation Context
- Expected touch points: `src/handlers/tool-result.ts`, a shared hashline-anchor helper, and tests under the M1.7 harness.
- Reuse the same anchor format consumed by the `hashline_edit` tool so search/edit workflows compose without translation.
- Performance matters because every successful `read` may pass through this path; keep the transformation linear and lightweight.

## Constraints
- Only rewrite text payloads; skip non-text/binary content blocks.
- Preserve `isError` responses verbatim.
- Skip if `cfg.hashline_edit` is false.
- Guard against malformed input shape — never throw.
- < 50ms average overhead per `tool_result` (PRD §6).
- Hashline scope is ONLY `read` and `write`; do NOT touch Pi's `edit` output.

## DoD
- Unit tests: happy path, `isError` preserved, non-text skipped, malformed input safe, perf microbenchmark assertion.
close_reason: Implemented: tool-result handler with hashline read rewrite + write normalize

title: M3.2: Hashline normalize of Pi 'write' tool output (line-count summary)
description:
## Objective
In `src/handlers/tool-result.ts`, when `event.toolName === "write"` and `!event.isError` and `cfg.hashline_edit === true`, replace the result content with a single text block: `"File written successfully. {N} lines written."`.

## Context
PRD §4.5 normalizes write output so the LLM does not get echoed file content after a successful write. This keeps context cleaner and aligns write behavior with the broader hashline workflow without modifying Pi's native edit tool semantics.

## Scope
IN:
- write branch of the tool-result handler
- line-count derivation from the write event payload
- tests for normalization and skip paths
OUT:
- read-result hashline rewrite (separate leaf)
- any changes to generic tool-result behavior for unrelated tools

## Implementation Context
- Expected touch points: `src/handlers/tool-result.ts`, any shared hashline normalization helper, and tests in the harness from M1.7.
- Derive the line count from the actual write payload/content, not from filesystem rereads.
- Keep the rewritten output shape stable because prompts may rely on this exact summary pattern.

## Constraints
- Skip if `hashline_edit` false.
- Preserve `isError`.
- Hashline scope ONLY read/write; never touch edit.
- Successful writes should summarize content, not echo it.

## DoD
- Unit tests: happy path, `isError` preserved, `hashline_edit=false` skipped.
- Test covers accurate line-count calculation for multi-line writes.
close_reason: Implemented: tool-result handler with hashline read rewrite + write normalize

title: M3.3: Inject <available_resources> + language-matching block in before_agent_start (idempotent)
description:
## Objective
Subscribe `before_agent_start`; build the `<available_resources>` block listing exactly the enabled-set (tools + skills + sub-agent personas: bytes, explore, oracle, librarian, general) and append it (plus the language-matching block) to `event.systemPrompt`. MUST be idempotent across turns.

## Context
PRD §4.6, §6 (idempotency), plan Decision 1 (single enabled-set) + Decision 6 (idempotent augmentation).

## Scope
IN: `src/handlers/before-agent-start.ts`. Wrap the block in a stable sentinel comment pair (e.g., `<!-- pi-blackbytes:resources:start -->` ... `<!-- pi-blackbytes:resources:end -->`). Detect the sentinel before appending; if present, replace the inner block instead of appending again.
OUT: model-select cache (separate leaf).

## Implementation Context
- Expected touch points: `src/handlers/before-agent-start.ts`, enabled-set reader from M1.6, any prompt-block builder helper, and tests.
- The rendered list must come from the single enabled-set so prompt-visible resources stay aligned with actual runtime availability.
- Keep replacement logic deterministic so repeated turns update the inner block without duplicating sentinel wrappers.

## Constraints
- Tool/persona names listed MUST match PRD §5.1 verbatim: `hashline_edit`, `ast_grep_search`, `ast_grep_replace`, `grep`, `glob`, `websearch_search`, `websearch_fetch`, `context7_resolve_library_id`, `context7_query_docs`, `grep_app_search_github`, `delegate_explore`, `delegate_oracle`, `delegate_librarian`, `delegate_general`.
- Reads from `getEnabledSet()` — never re-derive.
- Idempotent: repeated calls in the same session do not append duplicates.

## DoD
- Unit tests: first-turn appends, second-turn replaces in-place, disabled tool excluded, sentinel format stable.
close_reason: Available resources injection with sentinel-based idempotency

title: M3.4: Inject x-initiator: agent header for GitHub Copilot in before_provider_request
description:
## Objective
Subscribe `before_provider_request`; if `ctx.model.provider` is GitHub Copilot AND `cfg.copilot_initiator_header` is true, mutate the outgoing payload headers to add `x-initiator: agent`.

## Context
GitHub Copilot provider requests need the `x-initiator: agent` header in this workflow. The exact mutation mechanism is locked by M0.Q2, so this implementation bead should copy the confirmed payload shape instead of re-researching header semantics.

## Scope
IN:
- `src/handlers/before-provider-request.ts` header branch
- skip path when config disabled or provider not Copilot
- tests for add / skip / preserve-existing behavior
OUT:
- reasoning-effort mapping (next leaf)

## Implementation Context
- Reuse the provider-request event shape and mutation pattern recorded in `docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md`.
- Expected touch points: provider-request handler, shared model/provider helpers if needed, bootstrap wiring, and tests.
- Keep this header logic separate from the reasoning params branch so failures are easier to reason about.

## Constraints
- Only mutate when provider is Copilot.
- Never overwrite existing `x-initiator` if user-set.
- Failure must be caught + logged; not crash provider call.

## DoD
- Unit test: header added for copilot, not added otherwise, not overwritten if present.
- Implementation follows the exact mutation pattern confirmed by M0.Q2 rather than introducing a new transport wrapper.
close_reason: Copilot x-initiator header via registerProvider at session_start

title: M3.5: Cache active model family on model_select
description:
## Objective
Subscribe `model_select`; classify the active model into a family (`claude|gpt|gemini|other`) and cache it for use by the reasoning-effort mapper.

## Context
M3.6 needs a stable provider-family lookup so it can translate Blackbytes `reasoningEffort` settings into provider-specific request params. This bead owns the model-family state and invalidation rules, separate from the mutation logic itself.

## Scope
IN:
- `src/handlers/model-select.ts` with module-level cache
- family inference helper in `src/shared/model-capability.ts`
- tests for family classification and cache update behavior
OUT:
- actual provider-request param mutation (next leaf)

## Implementation Context
- The cached value should be readable by the `before_provider_request` handler without recomputing from scratch each time.
- Known families for MVP are Anthropic/Claude, OpenAI/GPT, Google/Gemini, plus a safe `other` bucket.
- Keep the helper reusable for other provider-capability checks if needed.

## Constraints
- Cache must be refreshed on every `model_select` event.
- Unknown model IDs should map to `other`, not throw.
- Avoid provider-specific network calls or heavy parsing; classification should be local and deterministic.

## DoD
- Unit test: family inference for known model IDs (`claude-*`, `gpt-*`, `gemini-*`).
- Test also covers an unknown model mapping to `other` and cache replacement on repeated `model_select` events.
close_reason: Implemented: model family classification + cache on model_select

title: M3.6: Map reasoningEffort to provider-family thinking/reasoning params in before_provider_request
description:
## Objective
In `src/handlers/before-provider-request.ts` (params branch), translate `reasoningEffort` (`low|medium|high`) into the correct provider-family params: Anthropic `thinking`, OpenAI `reasoning_effort`, Google `thinking_config`. Only intervene where Pi does not already handle it (PRD G6, ~90% parity).

## Context
Blackbytes exposes one cross-provider `reasoningEffort` setting, but actual request params vary by provider family. This leaf bridges that gap using the model-family cache from M3.5 while staying idempotent when Pi already injected equivalent settings.

## Scope
IN:
- param-mapping helper + handler glue
- detection of already-present provider-native params
- tests for per-family mapping and no-op paths
OUT:
- header mutation branch (separate leaf)

## Implementation Context
- Expected touch points: `src/handlers/before-provider-request.ts`, `src/shared/model-capability.ts`, and tests.
- Use the cached family from M3.5 rather than recomputing from raw model IDs in multiple places.
- Output/request mutations should stay narrowly scoped to the provider-native reasoning fields.

## Constraints
- Idempotent: don't double-set.
- Per-family logic in `src/shared/model-capability.ts`.
- Unsupported family should no-op safely.
- Do not clobber unrelated request params.

## DoD
- Unit tests: each family maps correctly; no-op when already set; unsupported family is skipped.
- Tests cover low/medium/high translation shape at the contract level rather than only one hard-coded value.
close_reason: Implemented

title: M3.7: Wire session_shutdown to flush logger
description:
## Objective
Subscribe `session_shutdown` and force any buffered logger state to flush before the extension exits.

## Context
This closes the logging lifecycle started in M1.3. Blackbytes keeps a buffered file logger for observability, so shutdown must not drop trailing entries when Pi tears down the session. PRD §5.2 expects logger writes to be durable across normal shutdown.

## Scope
IN:
- wire a `session_shutdown` handler
- invoke the shared logger flush path exactly once per shutdown event
- cover the shutdown path with a unit/integration-style test using the Pi mock harness
OUT:
- changing rotation, file paths, or normal append behavior from M1.3

## Implementation Context
- Expected touch points: `src/handlers/session-shutdown.ts` (or equivalent), logger module created in M1.3, bootstrap registration, and tests under the harness from M1.7.
- Reuse the existing logger abstraction; do not create a second shutdown-specific writer.
- If the logger exposes async flush semantics, the handler should await them before returning.

## Constraints
- Shutdown handling must be safe when logging is disabled or the logger has not been initialized.
- Multiple shutdown signals must not duplicate writes or throw.
- Failures should be swallowed/logged safely so teardown itself does not crash.

## DoD
- Unit test: shutdown flushes pending writes.
- Test also covers the no-op path when no logger is active or nothing is buffered.
- Registration path is explicit enough that later leaves can rely on flush-on-shutdown behavior without rereading the plan.
close_reason: Session shutdown flushes logger buffer

title: M4.0: Port persona prompts (bytes default/gpt/gemini, explore, oracle, librarian, general)
description:
## Objective
Port persona prompt markdowns from oc-blackbytes into `src/prompts/`:
- `bytes/{default,gpt,gemini}.md` (global system-prompt augmentation; not exposed as `delegate_*`)
- `explore.md`, `oracle.md`, `librarian.md`, `general.md` (used by `delegate_*` runner)

## Context
Blackbytes' delegation model depends on prompt assets that match each persona's role and runtime allowlist. The bytes family prompts shape the primary session behavior, while the sub-agent prompts are loaded by the nested runner for explore/oracle/librarian/general.

## Scope
IN:
- 7 markdown files under `src/prompts/`
- sub-agent prompts include the language-matching block and honest allowlist descriptions
- bytes prompt variants for default/gpt/gemini provider families
OUT:
- runtime loading/wiring of the prompts (separate leaves)

## Implementation Context
- Source material is oc-blackbytes; port the working content, then adjust names/tool references for this repo's canonical surface.
- Prompt text must stay consistent with M0.Q4 allowlists and M0.Q5 nested-runner contract.
- Keep file names and directory layout stable so later loader code can reference them deterministically.

## Constraints
- Each sub-agent prompt MUST describe its allowlist truthfully, even though enforcement happens at runtime.
- Do not expose the bytes prompts as delegate tools.
- Preserve language-matching behavior and project-operating style from the source prompts.

## DoD
- Files committed; load test reads them at runtime without error.
- Prompt set is complete enough that later delegate-tool leaves can wire it in without re-authoring persona guidance.
close_reason: Implemented: 7 persona prompt markdowns (bytes default/gpt/gemini + explore/oracle/librarian/general)

title: M4.1: Implement nested Pi runner (runNestedPi) per M0 strategy with cancellation, timeout, allowlist enforcement, recursion guard
description:
## Objective
Implement `src/sub-agents/runner.ts` exporting `runNestedPi({ systemPrompt, userPrompt, model, reasoningEffort, allowedTools, cwd, signal, onUpdate })` per the M0.Q1 chosen execution path (programmatic `AgentSession` or `pi -p` subprocess), with the M0.Q5 result/cancellation/timeout contract and the M0.Q4 runtime allowlist enforcement.

## Context
WS-F core. Plan Decisions 2 (runtime allowlist), 3 (`maxDepth=1`). Runner is consumed by all 4 `delegate_*` tools. Strategy and contract MUST follow `docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md` verbatim — do NOT improvise.

## Scope
IN: runner module, recursion guard (e.g., env var or context flag the runner sets so the spawned session does NOT register `delegate_*` tools), default timeout, parent-signal cancellation propagation, env inheritance allowlist, structured failure normalization (success: `{ content, details }`; failure: structured text + machine-readable details).
OUT: per-persona `delegate_*` tools (next leaves).

## Implementation Context
- Expected files: `src/sub-agents/runner.ts`, shared nested-result types/helpers, delegate-tool call sites, and tests.
- The runner should be the single place where timeout, cancellation, env inheritance, recursion depth, and allowlist rules are enforced so delegate leaves stay thin.
- Match the exact session strategy, error codes, and streaming/buffering policy recorded in the M0 decision doc.

## Constraints
- Allowlist enforced at RUNTIME, not just prompt (Decision 2). If subprocess path can't enforce, MUST fall back to programmatic per M0.Q1.
- `maxDepth = 1`: spawned sessions must not be able to call `delegate_*` again.
- Cancellation propagates from parent `AbortSignal`.
- `cwd` inherits from parent context; env inherits ONLY allowlisted vars per M0.Q5 record.
- Streaming optional; if unsupported, buffer and return single final result with same schema.

## Risks
- If chosen path leaks tools into the nested session beyond allowlist, fail loudly in tests.

## DoD
- Integration tests: success result, timeout, cancellation, allowlist-violation rejected, recursion-guard prevents nested `delegate_*` call.
close_reason: Nested Pi runner with recursion guard, timeout, allowlist, cancellation

title: M4.2: Implement delegate_explore tool (read/search only allowlist)
description:
## Objective
Implement `src/sub-agents/explore.ts` registering tool `delegate_explore` (canonical name from PRD §5.1). Spawns nested Pi via `runNestedPi` with `explore.md` system prompt and the explore allowlist.

## Context
PRD §4.4: explore allowlist = read/search only; NO write; NO shell/command execution. Tool name exact: `delegate_explore`. Skipped if `disabled_sub_agents` includes `"explore"`.

## Scope
IN: tool registration with params `{ question, ... }`, allowlist `['read', 'grep', 'glob', 'ast_grep_search', 'websearch_search', 'websearch_fetch']` (final list per M0.Q4 record; this is illustrative — re-derive from runtime tool registry intersecting with PRD §4.4 capability matrix).
OUT: runner internals (separate leaf).

## Implementation Context
- Expected files: `src/sub-agents/explore.ts`, shared delegate registration helpers, prompt assets from M4.0, and tests.
- Final allowlist should come from the M0.Q4 decision record intersected with the enabled-set from M1.6 rather than a duplicated static array.
- Result envelope, timeout, and recursion handling must follow the shared runner contract from M4.1 / M0.Q5.

## Constraints
- Allowlist is ENFORCED AT RUNTIME by runner — never rely on prompt alone.
- Reads from `getEnabledSet()` to skip if disabled.

## DoD
- Integration test: tool spawns nested session, returns result; attempting a write tool inside the session is blocked.
close_reason: Implemented

title: M4.3: Implement delegate_oracle tool (read-only consultation)
description:
## Objective
Implement `src/sub-agents/oracle.ts` registering tool `delegate_oracle` (canonical). Spawns nested Pi with `oracle.md` prompt, high reasoning effort default, and the oracle allowlist.

## Context
Oracle is the deep-reasoning consultation persona. It is read-only with no write or shell access, and web/docs access is allowed only when explicitly enabled by parent configuration. It should be the safe escalation path for hard debugging and architecture questions.

## Scope
IN:
- registration with params `{ question, context? }`
- runner call with `reasoningEffort: "high"` by default (overridable from cfg)
- runtime allowlist wiring for oracle-safe tools only
OUT:
- nested runner internals or prompt authoring

## Implementation Context
- Expected files: `src/sub-agents/oracle.ts`, shared delegate registration helpers, prompt assets from M4.0, and tests.
- Runtime allowlist and result envelope must follow M0.Q4–Q5 decisions exactly.
- Model selection should prefer `cfg.sub_agents.oracle` overrides first, then fall back to a sensible high-reasoning default.

## Constraints
- Tool name exact: `delegate_oracle`.
- Allowlist enforced at runtime.
- Consult `cfg.sub_agents.oracle` for model + `reasoningEffort` overrides.
- Nested `delegate_*` calls remain blocked by the recursion guard.

## DoD
- Integration test: spawn happy path; write tool invocation inside is blocked.
- Test also covers that optional web/docs tools are unavailable unless enabled by parent policy.
close_reason: Implemented

title: M4.4: Implement delegate_librarian tool (docs/web research only)
description:
## Objective
Implement `src/sub-agents/librarian.ts` registering tool `delegate_librarian` (canonical). Spawns nested Pi with `librarian.md` prompt and the librarian allowlist.

## Context
The librarian persona is for external-doc and open-source research. Unlike oracle it may use web/docs/code-search tools, but like explore it must stay read/search-only and never mutate the local workspace or run shell commands.

## Scope
IN:
- registration with question-style params
- allowlist includes `context7_*`, `websearch_*`, `grep_app_search_github`, plus local read/search tools (`read`, `grep`, `glob`, `ast_grep_search`)

## Implementation Context
- Expected files: `src/sub-agents/librarian.ts`, shared delegate registration helpers, prompt assets from M4.0, and tests.
- Runtime allowlist and result envelope must follow M0.Q4–Q5 decisions exactly.
- The tool should be usable both directly by users and as an internal delegation primitive from the Bytes persona.

## Constraints
- Tool name exact: `delegate_librarian`.
- NO write tools; NO local command execution.
- Web/docs tools are allowed only if enabled in the parent session's enabled-set/config.
- Nested `delegate_*` calls remain blocked by the recursion guard.

## DoD
- Integration test: spawn happy path; write/shell blocked.
- Test demonstrates that allowed docs/web/search tools remain available when enabled by parent policy.
close_reason: Implemented

title: M4.5: Implement delegate_general tool (full implementation agent)
description:
## Objective
Implement `src/sub-agents/general.ts` registering tool `delegate_general` (canonical). Spawns nested Pi with `general.md` prompt; full-tool allowlist (write + command execution) per parent session policy.

## Context
General is the heavy-lifting implementation persona. Unlike explore/oracle/librarian, it may write files and run local commands, but only within the enabled-set and recursion-safety boundaries defined by the parent session.

## Scope
IN:
- registration with params `{ task, context? }`
- allowlist = effectively all enabled tools EXCEPT `delegate_*` (recursion guard)

OUT:
- runner internals or persona prompt authoring

## Implementation Context

- Tool availability must derive from the enabled-set computed at session start, not a hard-coded list.
- Result envelope, timeout, env inheritance, and recursion handling must match the M0.Q5 decision record.

## Constraints

- `maxDepth=1` still applies: cannot call `delegate_*` again.
- Inherits cwd; env inheritance per M0.Q5 record.

## DoD

title: M2a: Bundled tools port (hashline, ast-grep, grep, glob)
description: Umbrella for WS-C. Port 5 bundled tools to Pi via pi.registerTool with @sinclair/typebox schemas. Reuse existing TS source where feasible. Respects 'disabled_tools' from settings.json blackbytes block. See plan §4 WS-C, §8 Slice 2.
close_reason: Epic umbrella — children unblocked

title: M2a.0: Shared TypeBox schema helpers + tool registration helper
description:
## Objective
Create the shared registration and schema helpers that every bundled/local tool implementation will build on: a guarded `registerTool` wrapper plus common TypeBox fragments for repeated parameter shapes.

## Context
This bead is the control point that prevents individual tool leaves from bypassing the enabled-set. It also keeps schema boilerplate consistent across the bundled tools port.

## Scope
IN:
- `src/tools/_shared/register-tool.ts` (or equivalent canonical path) that wraps `pi.registerTool`
- enabled-set gate based on the public tool name
- shared TypeBox helpers for repeated shapes such as paths, globs, grep-style search params, and filesystem target lists
- lightweight helper typing so later tool beads can register without duplicating guard logic
OUT:
- concrete tool implementations and HTTP client work

## Implementation Context
- Expected collaborators: enabled-set state from `M1.6`, future bundled tools under `src/tools/**`.
- The registration helper should define the narrow contract later beads use, e.g. name/schema/handler input and skip behavior when disabled.
- Shared schemas should be only the pieces reused across multiple tools; keep tool-specific schemas in their own beads.

## Constraints
- The helper must preserve the exact canonical tool names from the PRD.
- Disabled tools should be omitted cleanly rather than registered then hidden.
- Avoid over-abstracting; this is a small shared layer, not a framework.

## DoD
- Unit test proves disabled tools are skipped and enabled tools are registered.
- At least one shared TypeBox helper is used by more than one downstream tool family by design.
- The helper API is concrete enough that WS-C/WS-D leaves can implement against it without redefining the enabled-set check.
close_reason: Implemented: registerTool wrapper with enabled-set gate + shared TypeBox schema helpers

title: M2a.1: Port hashline_edit tool (LINE#ID-anchored multi-edit)
description:
## Objective
Implement `src/tools/hashline-edit/index.ts` registering tool `hashline_edit` (canonical name from PRD §5.1). Tool performs LINE#ID-anchored edits with replace/append/prepend operations, batching multiple edits per file in one call (snapshot semantics: bottom-up application).

## Context
Hashline edit is the central Bytes workflow. Reuse logic from existing oc-blackbytes `src/extensions/tools/hashline-edit/`. Schema via `@sinclair/typebox`; respect `disabled_tools`.

## Scope
IN: full tool implementation, edit operations (replace single/range, append/prepend with anchor or BOF/EOF), snapshot apply order, autocorrect for echoed prefixes/CRLF/BOM.
OUT: rewriting Pi's read tool output (that's the M3 hashline rewrite leaf).

## Implementation Context
- Expected files: `src/tools/hashline-edit/index.ts`, any hashline parser/apply helpers, shared registration/schema code from M2a.0, and tests.
- Match the existing hashline contract used by prompts and skills: LINE#ID anchors, bottom-up application, and precise range semantics.
- The tool should work with M3's read-output rewrite but remain independent from Pi's native `edit` implementation.

## Constraints
- Tool name MUST be exactly `hashline_edit` (PRD §5.1 canonical contract).
- Must NOT replace Pi's built-in `edit` tool (PRD §2.2 out-of-scope, plan Decision 5).
- Failure paths return structured error, never throw.

## DoD
- Unit tests: single-line replace, range replace, delete, append/prepend, BOF/EOF, multi-edit batch, hashline mismatch error.
close_reason: Implemented

title: M2a.2: Port ast_grep_search tool
description:
## Objective
Implement `src/tools/ast-grep/search.ts` registering tool `ast_grep_search` (canonical). Wraps the `ast-grep` binary for AST-aware pattern matching across 25 languages.

## Context
This is the structural-search counterpart to plain-text `grep`. It enables code-aware searches used by advanced refactor workflows and should port the proven oc-blackbytes behavior into this extension.

## Scope
IN:
- tool implementation
- `pattern` + `lang` + `paths`/`globs` params
- fail-gracefully behavior when the binary is missing
OUT:
- rewrite support (`ast_grep_replace`)

## Implementation Context
- Expected files: `src/tools/ast-grep/search.ts`, shared AST-grep helper(s), registration/schema code, and tests.
- Reuse oc-blackbytes behavior and installation guidance where appropriate.
- Structured error output should make it obvious how to install/fix the missing binary case.

## Constraints
- Tool name MUST be exactly `ast_grep_search`.
- Requires `ast-grep` binary in PATH; absence MUST not crash, only return error.
- Respect `disabled_tools` / enabled-set gating.

## DoD
- Unit test mocks binary; test missing-binary error path.
- Tool registers under the canonical name and returns stable search results/errors for downstream prompt use.
close_reason: Implemented

title: M2a.3: Port ast_grep_replace tool
description:
## Objective
Implement `src/tools/ast-grep/replace.ts` registering tool `ast_grep_replace` (canonical). AST-aware rewrite, `dryRun` by default.

## Context
This is the write-capable companion to `ast_grep_search`. It lets agents perform structural code rewrites with the same AST matcher semantics used for search, while defaulting to preview mode for safety.

## Scope
IN:
- tool with `pattern` + `rewrite` + `lang` + `paths`/`globs` + `dryRun` (default true)
- structured preview output for dry runs
- missing-binary and subprocess-error handling aligned with the search tool
OUT:
- generic text search or hashline editing

## Implementation Context
- Expected files: `src/tools/ast-grep/replace.ts`, shared AST-grep helper(s), registration/schema code, and tests.
- Reuse the same binary-detection strategy as `ast_grep_search` so both tools fail consistently.
- Output should help an implementer decide whether to rerun with `dryRun=false`.

## Constraints
- `dryRun` default true (safety).
- Requires `ast-grep` binary; same fallback/error behavior as search.
- Public contract must stay separate from `hashline_edit`; this tool is for AST-pattern rewrites.

## DoD
- Unit test: `dryRun` produces preview, missing binary returns clean error.
- Tool registers under the canonical name and shares binary-error semantics with the search leaf.
close_reason: Implemented

title: M2a.4: Port grep tool (ripgrep with Node fallback)
description:
## Objective
Implement `src/tools/grep/index.ts` registering tool `grep` (canonical). Prefer `ripgrep` binary if present; fall back to a Node implementation. Modes: `content` / `files_with_matches` / `count`.

## Context
This is the main local content-search primitive and one of the most-used Blackbytes tools. It should behave consistently across environments while still taking advantage of `ripgrep` when available for speed.

## Scope
IN:
- tool params `pattern`, `include`, `output_mode`, `head_limit`, `path`
- automatic `ripgrep` detection with Node fallback
- output shaping for all three modes
OUT:
- file-name matching (`glob`) and AST-aware search (`ast_grep_search`)

## Implementation Context
- Expected files: `src/tools/grep/index.ts`, shared registration/schema helpers from M2a.0, fallback implementation code, and tests.
- Reuse oc-blackbytes behavior where appropriate, especially around caps and output structure.
- Keep output compatible with downstream prompts expecting concise match lists or content snippets.

## Constraints
- Tool name exact: `grep`.
- Fallback path must preserve the same public contract as the `ripgrep` path.
- Invalid regex / command failures must return structured errors rather than crash.

## DoD
- Unit tests for each output mode; fallback path test.
- Coverage includes both `ripgrep`-present and fallback semantics at the contract level.
close_reason: Implemented grep and glob tools with tests; all new tests passing

title: M2a.5: Port glob tool
description:
## Objective
Implement `src/tools/glob/index.ts` registering tool `glob` (canonical). Uses `fast-glob`, returns matching paths sorted by mtime, capped at 100 with 60s timeout.

## Context
This ports one of the core local-search tools from oc-blackbytes. It gives agents a safe file-discovery primitive that is faster and narrower than shelling out to `find`, and it is part of the default read/search workflow expected by prompts and skills.

## Scope
IN:
- tool with `pattern` + `path` params
- mtime-desc sorting of matches
- 100-file cap and 60s timeout behavior
- structured error handling for invalid paths/timeouts
OUT:
- content search (`grep`) or AST search behavior

## Implementation Context
- Expected files: `src/tools/glob/index.ts`, any shared schema/registration helper from M2a.0, and unit tests.
- Reuse logic from oc-blackbytes where sensible, but keep dependency usage aligned with this repo's package/runtime choices.
- Returned paths should be suitable for later `read` calls.

## Constraints
- Tool name exact: `glob`.
- Preserve the 100-match safety cap and timeout even when the underlying library could return more.
- Sort by mtime so the most recently touched files appear first.

## DoD
- Unit tests: glob match, mtime sort, cap, timeout error.
- Tool output and failure mode are specific enough for downstream prompts to rely on without shell fallback.
close_reason: Implemented grep and glob tools with tests; all new tests passing

title: Refactor Blackbytes system prompt into runtime-backed v2 overlay
description:
Objective: Deliver a v2 system-prompt architecture that replaces the current large appended Bytes prompt with a compact, runtime-backed policy overlay.

Context: The current prompt is effective but too large, too invasive, and only loosely synchronized with runtime state. We want a design that is shorter, more trustworthy, more stable across model families, and easier to test.

Scope: Includes prompt architecture, runtime capability-aware rendering, injection flow updates, tests, and documentation. Does not include unrelated tooling or broad agent-behavior rewrites outside the Blackbytes prompt pipeline.

Components touched: src/prompts/bytes/*, src/prompts/loader.ts, src/handlers/before-agent-start.ts, src/shared/model-capability.ts, src/config/resource-metadata.ts, prompt tests, README.md, AGENTS.md.

Important assumptions and constraints: The v2 overlay must defer to PI host/system rules, user requirements, and project instructions; model-family adapters should affect formatting, not semantics; prompt sections should only mention capabilities that are actually enabled at runtime.

Validation / Definition of Done: The repository has a reviewed bead graph covering prompt architecture, runtime integration, tests, and docs, with clear dependencies and enough context for an AI implementer to work without the original discussion.
close_reason: All children closed: prompt architecture (1), runtime integration (2), tests and docs (3). V2 overlay fully implemented.

title: Build canonical prompt overlay architecture
description:
Objective: Define and implement one semantic source of truth for the Blackbytes v2 prompt overlay.

Context: Prompt meaning is currently spread across shared prose blobs and family-specific variants. Consolidating semantics is the foundation for reducing drift, token bloat, and model-family inconsistency.

Scope: Covers typed render context, semantic section builders, and formatting-only model adapters. Does not cover runtime injection wiring, metadata enrichment, or tests outside what is needed to support the architecture.

Components touched: src/prompts/bytes/shared.ts, src/prompts/bytes/default.ts, src/prompts/bytes/gpt.ts, src/prompts/bytes/gemini.ts, new bytes prompt support files.

Dependencies / Prerequisites: Child work should complete before runtime integration and test hardening branches can finish.

Important assumptions and constraints: The overlay should be compact; sections must be layered as precedence, capabilities, hard boundaries, defaults, conditional workflows, and completion contract; adapters must not introduce semantic drift.

Validation / Definition of Done: The architecture branch has concrete child beads that together produce a typed, renderable, model-stable prompt overlay.
close_reason: All children closed: typed render context (1.1), canonical overlay builder (1.2), formatting-only adapters (1.3). Architecture fully implemented.

title: Introduce typed prompt render context and section model
description:
Objective: Add typed structures for prompt render context, feature flags, and renderable prompt sections.

Context: The current prompt path is string-heavy and spreads runtime assumptions across multiple files. A typed model is needed so overlay generation becomes explicit, testable, and easy to extend.

Scope: Define the core interfaces/types for model family, enabled tools, enabled sub-agents, derived feature flags, and prompt sections. Do not yet move all prompt wording or injection logic in this bead.

Components touched: new src/prompts/bytes/types.ts; small supporting changes in src/prompts/loader.ts or adjacent helpers if needed for type adoption.

Dependencies / Prerequisites: Starts from the existing prompt pipeline; no parent-child child beads need to finish first.

Important assumptions and constraints: Types must reflect real runtime data, avoid circular coupling with renderer code, and be ergonomic for both semantic builders and model adapters.

Risks / Gotchas: Over-designing the type model can slow later implementation; keep it minimal and centered on current v2 prompt needs.

Validation / Definition of Done: Types compile cleanly, are imported by the new overlay/render code, and clearly express the context needed for capability-aware prompt rendering.
acceptance_criteria:
- `src/prompts/bytes/types.ts` defines the shared types needed for v2 prompt rendering, including render context, feature flags, and prompt sections.
- The type model supports enabled tools, enabled sub-agents, and model-family-aware rendering without requiring stringly-typed ad hoc objects.
- Any touched prompt loader/helpers compile cleanly against the new types.
- Validation: `bun run build` passes.
notes: Out of scope: moving prompt wording, changing injection behavior, or rewriting model-family variants. Keep this bead limited to types and any minimal plumbing required to adopt them.
close_reason: Already implemented: types.ts contains BytesPromptRenderContext, PromptFeatureFlags, PromptSection, PromptSectionMap, PromptSectionKey, PromptRenderer, PromptVariantRenderContext, PromptBuilder. Build passes cleanly.

title: Implement canonical semantic overlay builder
description:
Objective: Build one semantic source of truth that generates the v2 overlay sections from prompt render context.

Context: The current shared prompt prose mixes facts, hard rules, defaults, and heuristics in large static blobs. The v2 design requires section builders that separate precedence, capabilities, hard boundaries, defaults, conditional workflows, and completion behavior.

Scope: Add overlay builder code (for example in src/prompts/bytes/overlay.ts) and migrate the prompt meaning out of the old shared prose blobs. Do not yet finalize before_agent_start wiring or tests beyond what is needed to support the builder.

Components touched: new overlay builder file(s), src/prompts/bytes/shared.ts replacement/removal, supporting prompt exports.

Dependencies / Prerequisites: Depends on the typed prompt render context bead.

Important assumptions and constraints: Keep the overlay compact; only render conditional sections when the corresponding capability is enabled; phrase hard boundaries strongly and heuristics softly.

Risks / Gotchas: Carrying forward too much of the current verbose wording will undermine the token-economy goal; be willing to cut low-ROI prose.

Validation / Definition of Done: A single semantic builder can produce all v2 sections from context, with no semantic prompt logic left duplicated across model-family variants.
acceptance_criteria:
- A canonical semantic builder exists (for example `src/prompts/bytes/overlay.ts`) and emits the full v2 section set from render context.
- Section logic clearly separates precedence, session capabilities, hard boundaries, work defaults, conditional workflows, and completion contract.
- Prompt semantics are no longer duplicated across family-specific prompt files.
- Validation: `bun run build` passes and the rendered sections are usable by thin adapters.
notes: Out of scope: wiring the overlay into `before_agent_start`, updating docs, or adding final snapshot coverage unless minimal scaffolding is needed for the builder itself.
close_reason: Already implemented: overlay.ts contains canonical semantic builder (buildBytesPromptOverlay) producing all 6 v2 sections from render context. Family variants are formatting-only adapters. No semantic duplication.

title: Convert model-family variants into formatting-only adapters
description:
Objective: Rewrite the Claude/default, GPT, and Gemini prompt variant files so they format shared sections rather than defining separate prompt semantics.

Context: Family-specific full prompts are a major source of drift and unpredictability. v2 should keep one meaning and let adapters change only presentation details such as headers or spacing.

Scope: Refactor src/prompts/bytes/default.ts, gpt.ts, gemini.ts, and src/prompts/loader.ts to render the same semantic sections through thin adapters. Do not reintroduce variant-specific policy text.

Components touched: src/prompts/bytes/default.ts, src/prompts/bytes/gpt.ts, src/prompts/bytes/gemini.ts, src/prompts/loader.ts, renderer support code.

Dependencies / Prerequisites: Depends on the canonical semantic overlay builder.

Important assumptions and constraints: All adapters must preserve meaning, section order, and conditional inclusion; differences should be limited to formatting preferences per model family.

Risks / Gotchas: It is easy to leave accidental wording differences behind; review final outputs side by side.

Validation / Definition of Done: Switching model family changes formatting only; semantic content and capability gating remain equivalent across variants.
acceptance_criteria:
- `src/prompts/bytes/default.ts`, `gpt.ts`, and `gemini.ts` become formatting-only adapters over shared rendered sections.
- `src/prompts/loader.ts` selects adapters without reintroducing prompt semantics into variant files.
- Equivalent sessions across model families differ only in presentation, not meaning or capability gating.
- Validation: `bun run build` passes.
notes: Out of scope: changing runtime feature gating or adding documentation. Review side-by-side outputs to ensure no accidental semantic drift remains.
close_reason: Already implemented: default.ts, gemini.ts, gpt.ts are formatting-only adapters over shared PromptSectionMap. loader.ts selects by family without reintroducing semantics. No variant-specific policy text.

title: Ground overlay in runtime state and injection flow
description:
Objective: Make prompt rendering depend on actual session capabilities and a reliable injection path instead of static prose assumptions.

Context: The current prompt can describe unavailable behavior and depends on fragile state such as model-family caching. Runtime grounding is the main trustworthiness improvement in v2.

Scope: Covers capability derivation, metadata-driven feature gating, model-family resolution, and before_agent_start integration. Does not cover snapshot/sync tests or documentation refresh except where needed to support the runtime changes.

Components touched: src/handlers/before-agent-start.ts, src/handlers/index.ts, src/shared/model-capability.ts, src/config/resource-metadata.ts, prompt render path.

Dependencies / Prerequisites: Depends on the canonical overlay architecture being available.

Important assumptions and constraints: Missing state must degrade to a minimal safe overlay rather than no augmentation; runtime-rendered sections must never mention disabled tools or agents.

Validation / Definition of Done: before_agent_start renders the v2 overlay from runtime state with stable model-family selection and feature-aware sections.
close_reason: All 3 children (2.1, 2.2, 2.3) closed — runtime-grounded overlay architecture complete

title: Enrich resource metadata for prompt feature gating
description:
Objective: Extend resource metadata so prompt rendering can derive feature flags and concise capability guidance from one source of truth.

Context: The current prompt hardcodes too much knowledge about tools and agents. Metadata-driven rendering reduces false affordances and makes prompt/runtime synchronization testable.

Scope: Add prompt-facing metadata for tools, tool groups, or sub-agents where needed to derive features such as hashline editing, delegation availability, docs lookup, and GitHub code search. Keep the metadata focused on rendering needs.

Components touched: src/config/resource-metadata.ts and any nearby helper code that derives prompt-facing capability flags.

Dependencies / Prerequisites: Should align with the typed render context and semantic overlay architecture.

Important assumptions and constraints: Do not create a giant second ontology; add only the metadata needed to render grounded prompt sections and keep public tool names authoritative.

Risks / Gotchas: Duplicating knowledge that already exists elsewhere can create new drift; prefer references to existing tool names over parallel taxonomies.

Validation / Definition of Done: Prompt render code can derive its capability/feature view from resource metadata and enabled sets without hardcoding scattered tool/agent assumptions.
acceptance_criteria:
- `src/config/resource-metadata.ts` exposes the prompt-facing metadata needed to derive feature flags such as hashline editing, delegation, docs lookup, and GitHub code search.
- Production prompt rendering can derive its feature view from authoritative metadata plus enabled sets rather than scattered hardcoded assumptions.
- Public tool and sub-agent names remain the single source of truth.
- Validation: `bun run build` passes.
notes: Out of scope: broad metadata redesign for unrelated systems. Keep additions narrowly focused on prompt rendering and capability gating.
close_reason: Already implemented: resource-metadata.ts has promptFeatures on ToolMeta/ToolGroupMeta/SubAgentMeta and derivePromptFeatureFlags() derives flags from metadata+enabled sets

title: Harden model-family resolution for prompt rendering
description:
Objective: Remove or reduce reliance on fragile module-global model-family state so prompt adapters are selected consistently.

Context: before_agent_start can run before model_select updates the cached family, causing the wrong prompt adapter to be chosen. v2 needs deterministic behavior even when session events arrive in different orders.

Scope: Refactor src/shared/model-capability.ts and any related call sites so prompt rendering receives reliable model-family data or falls back safely. Do not broaden this bead into unrelated model policy changes.

Components touched: src/shared/model-capability.ts, src/handlers/index.ts, prompt loader/render call sites.

Dependencies / Prerequisites: Works best once the new overlay/render path exists.

Important assumptions and constraints: If model-family information is unavailable, degrade to a documented safe default rather than silently producing semantically different prompt behavior.

Risks / Gotchas: Session-scoped vs module-scoped state choices can affect other codepaths; keep the change narrow and review for regressions.

Validation / Definition of Done: Prompt rendering chooses the intended adapter consistently, including startup/fallback scenarios that previously depended on timing.
acceptance_criteria:
- Prompt rendering no longer depends on fragile timing of a module-global cached model family.
- Startup and fallback paths choose a deterministic adapter or documented safe default.
- Any touched handlers or helpers preserve existing behavior outside prompt-family selection.
- Validation: `bun run build` passes and targeted tests/fixtures can exercise the fallback path.
notes: Out of scope: broader model-selection UX or provider reasoning policy changes. Keep this bead focused on reliable family resolution for prompt rendering.
close_reason: Already implemented: resolvePromptModelFamily() provides deterministic fallback (classify from modelId > cached family > DEFAULT_PROMPT_MODEL_FAMILY) and before-agent-start uses it

title: Refactor before_agent_start to render capability-aware v2 overlay
description:
Objective: Replace the current large Bytes augmentation block with the new compact v2 overlay rendered from runtime context.

Context: This is the integration point where prompt architecture becomes real session behavior. It must gather enabled tools, enabled sub-agents, derived features, and model-family information, then inject the sentinel-delimited overlay reliably.

Scope: Update src/handlers/before-agent-start.ts and related call paths to build prompt render context, render the overlay, preserve idempotent sentinel replacement, and provide a minimal safe fallback when state is incomplete.

Components touched: src/handlers/before-agent-start.ts, src/handlers/index.ts, prompt loader/render integration, any helper used to derive context.

Dependencies / Prerequisites: Depends on the semantic overlay builder, formatting adapters, metadata enrichment, and hardened model-family resolution.

Important assumptions and constraints: The injected overlay must defer to project instructions, only mention available capabilities, and never fail closed into no augmentation when a conservative fallback is possible.

Risks / Gotchas: Regressing sentinel replacement or feature gating would make the prompt less stable than today; preserve existing idempotent update behavior.

Validation / Definition of Done: before_agent_start injects the v2 overlay in all supported sessions, with correct conditional sections and safe fallback behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
- `src/handlers/before-agent-start.ts` builds prompt render context from runtime state and injects the v2 overlay using the existing sentinel replacement pattern.
- The injected overlay only mentions enabled capabilities and falls back to a minimal safe overlay if runtime state is incomplete.
- `src/handlers/index.ts` and adjacent helpers still wire prompt injection correctly for normal sessions.
- Validation: `bun run build` passes and a rendered overlay can be inspected in tests/fixtures.
notes: Out of scope: rewriting unrelated session handlers or changing tool registration behavior. Preserve idempotent replacement and keep failure behavior conservative.
close_reason: Already implemented: before-agent-start.ts builds render context from runtime state, renders v2 overlay via renderBytesPrompt, uses sentinel idempotent replacement, falls back to minimal overlay when EnabledSet unavailable, only mentions enabled capabilities

title: Add prompt reliability tests and docs
description:
Objective: Lock in the v2 prompt behavior with tests and updated docs so prompt/runtime drift is caught early.

Context: Prompt quality is not trustworthy long-term unless the repository validates rendered output, capability gating, and public documentation.

Scope: Covers snapshot-style overlay tests, capability sync tests, fallback coverage, and documentation updates. Does not redesign the overlay itself or change unrelated project docs.

Components touched: src/prompts/__tests__/*, README.md, AGENTS.md, any prompt-test utilities needed.

Dependencies / Prerequisites: Depends on the overlay architecture and runtime integration work reaching a stable shape.

Important assumptions and constraints: Tests should verify semantics, conditional section rendering, and prompt/runtime consistency without hardcoding brittle incidental formatting beyond what the adapters intentionally define.

Validation / Definition of Done: Tests fail on prompt/runtime drift and the docs describe the new capability-aware overlay accurately.

title: Add overlay rendering snapshots and conditional-section tests
description:
Objective: Add tests that prove the v2 overlay renders correctly across common capability combinations and model families.

Context: The new architecture is only trustworthy if we can catch regressions in section order, fallback behavior, and conditional inclusion. Snapshot-style coverage is the fastest way to make prompt evolution visible.

Scope: Add tests for full-capability sessions, disabled sub-agents, disabled hashline_edit, missing docs-search features, and minimal fallback rendering. Include cross-family checks that semantics stay aligned even if formatting differs.

Components touched: new src/prompts/__tests__/bytes-overlay.test.ts and any supporting test fixtures/helpers.

Dependencies / Prerequisites: Depends on the runtime-integrated v2 overlay.

Important assumptions and constraints: Assertions should focus on intended semantics and gating behavior; avoid brittle snapshots of incidental whitespace that adapters do not promise to keep stable.

Risks / Gotchas: Over-brittle snapshots can create churn; structure the tests so failures highlight meaningful prompt changes.

Validation / Definition of Done: Test coverage fails when sections appear/disappear incorrectly, fallback behavior regresses, or model-family semantics drift.
acceptance_criteria:
- Test coverage exists for full-capability rendering, disabled sub-agents, disabled `hashline_edit`, missing docs-search capabilities, and minimal fallback rendering.
- Tests verify section inclusion/exclusion and semantic stability across model families without depending on incidental formatting noise.
- Failures clearly indicate meaningful overlay regressions.
- Validation: `bun run test` passes.
notes: Out of scope: exhaustive golden tests for every whitespace difference. Prefer focused snapshots or assertions that protect intended behavior.
close_reason: Already implemented: bytes-overlay.test.ts has 5 tests covering full-capability, disabled sub-agents, disabled hashline_edit, missing docs-search, and minimal fallback rendering across model families

title: Add prompt-to-runtime capability sync tests
description:
Objective: Add tests that ensure prompt text and rendered capability sections stay synchronized with registered tool and sub-agent metadata.

Context: A major v2 goal is reducing false affordances. Sync tests catch drift where prompts mention unavailable or renamed capabilities before that reaches users.

Scope: Add tests that verify prompt-visible tool/agent references come from authoritative metadata, feature flags derive from real enabled resources, and unavailable capabilities are not rendered.

Components touched: new src/prompts/__tests__/bytes-capability-sync.test.ts and any small helper code needed to inspect metadata/rendered output.

Dependencies / Prerequisites: Depends on metadata-driven rendering and the v2 injection path being in place.

Important assumptions and constraints: Keep the tests close to the public resource metadata so future renames fail fast.

Risks / Gotchas: If tests encode a parallel list of tool names, they can themselves drift; derive expectations from the same metadata exported by production code wherever possible.

Validation / Definition of Done: Renaming or disabling a prompt-facing capability causes tests to fail unless the rendered overlay and metadata are updated together.
acceptance_criteria:
- Tests verify that prompt-visible capabilities come from authoritative resource metadata rather than stray hardcoded names.
- Disabling or renaming a prompt-facing tool/sub-agent causes the sync tests to fail unless render logic and metadata are updated together.
- Feature flags used by prompt rendering are derived from production metadata/helpers, not a duplicate list local to the tests.
- Validation: `bun run test` passes.
notes: Out of scope: testing unrelated runtime metadata consumers. Keep the focus on prompt/runtime synchronization and false-affordance prevention.
close_reason: Already implemented: bytes-capability-sync.test.ts has 6 tests verifying metadata-backed resources, feature flag derivation, and per-capability rendering sync

title: M5.1: Implement /setup-models wizard (atomic safe write to settings.json)
description:
## Objective
Implement `src/commands/setup-models.ts` registering `pi.registerCommand("setup-models", ...)`. TUI wizard using `ctx.ui.select/input/confirm` to:
- detect / pick provider(s),
- collect API keys (Exa or Tavily for websearch, Context7 optional, Copilot/OpenAI/Anthropic provider keys as relevant),
- pick default model and recommended reasoning settings,
- write `~/.pi/agent/settings.json` (or `PI_AGENT_DIR/settings.json`) preserving all unrelated keys.

## Context
Plan Decision 4 + PRD §6 Config safety. JSON only, no JSONC. Atomic write (write to temp file then rename) so concurrent Pi reads never see partial files. Must dedupe `packages` array entries. Confirm before overwriting any existing key in the `blackbytes` block.

## Scope
IN: wizard flow, merge algorithm for `blackbytes` block, atomic write helper, dedupe packages, JSON validation pre-write, no-secret logging.
OUT: `/blackbytes-status` (separate optional leaf).

## Implementation Context
- Expected files: `src/commands/setup-models.ts`, config read/write helpers, logger/redaction reuse, and tests.
- The command should read the latest settings immediately before merge so interactive changes do not clobber unrelated edits.
- Keep provider/model questions aligned with the actual config schema from 0d5.4 and the enabled external-tool surface.

## Constraints
- JSON only — must NOT emit JSONC.
- Preserve all keys outside `blackbytes` byte-for-byte.
- Atomic write: temp file + `fs.rename`.
- Re-read settings immediately before merge to minimize lost-update window.
- Never log secret values entered by user (use redaction from logger).
- Confirm prompt before overwriting any existing `blackbytes.*` key.
- Validate `JSON.stringify` result parses back successfully before writing.

## Risks
- Settings clobber on concurrent edit — mitigated by re-read + atomic rename.

## DoD
- Integration tests: missing file (creates), malformed file (notify + abort, do not destroy), concurrent edit (re-reads), key preservation, secret-not-logged assertion, dedupe packages, confirm-before-overwrite branch.
close_reason: Implemented: setup-models wizard with atomic write, confirm-before-overwrite, dedupe packages

title: M6.6: CI gates — perf and size budgets
description:
## Objective
Add CI checks enforcing performance and size budgets per PRD §6 NFRs.

- `scripts/measure-tool-result.ts` — runs N iterations of the `tool_result` handler chain (22a.1+22a.2) over a representative payload, reports average overhead.
- `scripts/check-size.sh` — `npm pack` then `gzip -c <tarball> | wc -c` and assert < 500KB.

OUT: dashboards, historical tracking.

- Startup benchmark should exercise the actual bootstrap wiring under the Pi mock from M1.7, not an isolated toy path.
- Size check should measure the publishable tarball, not the raw repo checkout.

## Constraints
- Budgets are HARD gates; do NOT introduce a "warn-only" tier.
- Microbenchmarks must run in <30s total to keep CI fast.

## DoD
- CI fails when any budget exceeded; passes on the current MVP build.

title: M6.7: CI matrix against pinned latest @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent
description:

## Scope

OUT: cross-OS matrix (Linux only is fine for MVP).

## Implementation Context
- Expected touch points: `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, package install/bootstrap steps, and any helper env needed for the test harness.
- The Pi-version axis should install explicit npm versions rather than relying on a floating tag.
- Keep the workflow compatible with the integration harness from M1.7 and the test suites added by swh.1–swh.5.

## Constraints
- Pin BOTH Pi versions in the matrix (do not float "latest") so CI is reproducible.

title: M0: Feasibility spike — lock nested-session, header mutation, active-tools, allowlist, transport contracts
description: Umbrella for M0 feasibility spike. Resolves PRD §9 Q1–Q3 plus allowlist enforcement and transport/cancellation contract.
