close_reason: Already implemented

title: M0.Q1: Choose nested Pi session execution path (programmatic AgentSession vs subprocess)
description:
## Objective
Decide the nested-session execution path for `delegate_*` tools: programmatic `AgentSession` from `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` (target `^0.67`) versus a `pi -p` subprocess fallback. Record the final decision in `docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md` under `Q1: nested-session execution`.

## Context
This bead is the implementation contract for WS-F's future nested runner (`M4.1`). The chosen path must support runtime tool allowlist enforcement, parent-driven cancellation, and a hard recursion limit (`maxDepth=1`). If one path cannot satisfy those constraints cleanly, reject it and document the fallback only as contingency.

## Scope
IN:
- inspect exported APIs/types from `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent`
- probe whether programmatic sessions can enforce tool subsets and propagate `AbortSignal`
- compare against subprocess behavior for startup cost, streaming support, cancellation, and allowlist control
- write the decision record and fallback path in `docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md`
OUT:
- implementing the runner, delegate tools, or prompt text

## Implementation Context
- Expected research surface: package exports/types, any existing nested-session helpers, and a tiny throwaway probe if source inspection is inconclusive.
- Downstream consumers: `src/subagents/run-nested-pi.ts` (future), delegate tool handlers, and the allowlist design locked in `M0.Q4`.
- Capture the exact API or subprocess invocation shape that M4 should implement.

## Constraints
- Target Pi version: `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent ^0.67`.
- Runtime allowlist enforcement is mandatory; prompt-only enforcement is forbidden.
- Cancellation must propagate from parent `AbortSignal`.
- Nested sessions must not be able to invoke another `delegate_*` tool (`maxDepth=1`).

## DoD
- `docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md` contains a `Q1: nested-session execution` section with:
  - chosen path
  - rejected alternative and why
  - fallback path
  - allowlist enforcement plan
  - cancellation contract
  - streaming policy (`stream` vs buffered)
  - any startup/perf notes relevant to M4
- The note includes enough concrete API detail that M4 can proceed without rereading the original plan.
close_reason: Research: programmatic createAgentSession chosen over subprocess. SDK provides tool allowlist via tools param, cancellation via session.abort(), in-process with no startup overhead. Documented in docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md Q1.

title: M0.Q2: Confirm before_provider_request can mutate HTTP headers (Copilot x-initiator)
description:
## Objective
Confirm whether Pi's `before_provider_request` hook can mutate outgoing HTTP headers, not just body/params, and record the exact mutation pattern in `docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md` under `Q2: header mutation`.

## Context
WS-E uses this for the GitHub Copilot `x-initiator: agent` header (`M3.4`) and for future provider-request mutations. The goal is to lock the actual event payload shape before implementation, including whether header maps are mutable in place or require replacement.

## Scope
IN:
- inspect Pi types/source for `before_provider_request`
- run a minimal proof-of-capability probe if the type surface is ambiguous
- document the exact payload shape and mutation pattern
- define the fallback path if header mutation is unsupported
OUT:
- production handler implementation in `src/handlers/before-provider-request.ts`

## Implementation Context
- Decision doc target: `docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md`
- Future consumer: `M3.4` must be able to copy a working snippet directly from this bead output.
- Capture whether headers are plain objects, `Headers`, or provider-specific structures, and whether mutation happens preflight or via replacement.

## Constraints
- Focus on request-header mutation only; do not broaden into unrelated provider-request logic.
- If header mutation is unsupported, document the minimum viable fallback (for example a custom transport wrapper) and what complexity it adds.

## DoD
- Decision section states `confirmed: yes|no`.
- Includes exact payload shape, one working mutation snippet, and any caveats (e.g. immutable objects, provider-specific fields, timing).
- Explicitly states the implementation rule M3.4 should follow and the fallback if unsupported.
close_reason: Research confirmed: before_provider_request cannot mutate HTTP headers (payload-only). Fallback: pi.registerProvider with headers option. Decision documented in docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md Q2.

title: M0.Q3: Confirm pi.setActiveTools dynamism semantics
description:
## Objective
Determine whether `pi.setActiveTools` (or an equivalent Pi API) can safely change the active tool set after `session_start`, or whether the extension must compute the enabled-set once and only register/expose that subset for the entire session. Record the answer in `docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md` under `Q3: active-tools dynamism`.

## Context
This locks Decision 1 for WS-A. `before_agent_start`, `resources_discover`, `<available_resources>`, tool registration, and `/blackbytes-status` must all read from one shared enabled-set. If post-start mutation is weak or cosmetic, the session_start-only strategy wins.

## Scope
IN:
- inspect the `ExtensionAPI` surface related to tool activation
- test both strategies if the API appears ambiguous:
  1. register all then dynamically narrow active tools
  2. compute enabled-set at `session_start` and register/expose only that subset
- document implications for tool registration and resource exposure
OUT:
- implementing the enabled-set module itself (`M1.6`)

## Implementation Context
- Downstream consumer: `src/session/enabled-set.ts` (future) plus bootstrap wiring.
- Capture whether dynamism affects only model-visible resources, only invocation permission, or both.
- The decision must be specific enough that `M1.6` can implement the correct strategy without re-research.

## Constraints
- Single source of truth is mandatory; no split between visible resources and invocable tools.
- MVP does not need mid-session enable/disable support unless Pi provides it cleanly and consistently.

## DoD
- Decision section states `dynamic: yes|no`.
- Includes chosen strategy, why the alternative was rejected, and the concrete implication for `resources_discover` and registration timing.
- Notes any API caveats or tests used to confirm behavior.
close_reason: Confirmed dynamic: yes. pi.setActiveTools() supports post-session_start tool toggling. Strategy: register all at startup, narrow with setActiveTools(). Decision documented in docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md Q3.

title: M0.Q4: Define runtime tool-allowlist enforcement strategy for nested sessions
description:
## Objective
Define the runtime enforcement mechanism for persona-specific tool allowlists in nested sessions and document it in `docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md` under `Q4: nested-session allowlist enforcement`.

## Context
Prompt-only enforcement is explicitly forbidden. `delegate_explore`, `delegate_oracle`, `delegate_librarian`, and `delegate_general` each need a runtime-enforced capability boundary before `M4.1` can be implemented safely.

## Scope
IN:
- pick the enforcement point that matches the Q1 session strategy (tool registration subset, invocation interception, child config, or equivalent)
- document the exact allowlist per persona
- define what happens on violation and what gets returned to the parent session
OUT:
- implementing the nested runner or delegate tools

## Implementation Context
- Future implementation areas: nested runner setup, tool registration helper, delegate tool wrappers.
- The decision must state whether enforcement happens by omission (tool never registered), denial at call time, or both.
- Include the structured error shape or response contract used when a child tries a forbidden tool.

## Constraints
- Preserve the MVP persona matrix exactly:
  - `delegate_explore`: read/search only; no write; no shell
  - `delegate_oracle`: read-only consultation; no write; no shell; web/docs only if explicitly enabled
  - `delegate_librarian`: read/search/docs/web only; no write; no shell
  - `delegate_general`: full implementation; can write/shell per parent policy
- Prompt text may reinforce policy but cannot be the only defense.

## DoD
- Decision section includes:
  - chosen enforcement mechanism
  - persona allowlist matrix verbatim
  - violation behavior and structured error contract
  - confirmation that prompt-only enforcement is not relied on
  - any limitations that M4 must preserve
close_reason: Research: enforcement by tool registration omission + beforeToolCall interception. Persona allowlist matrix defined. Prompt-only enforcement rejected. Documented in docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md Q4.

title: M0.Q5: Define result transport, cancellation, timeout, and recursion-guard contract for delegate_* tools
description:
## Objective
Lock the execution contract for every `delegate_*` tool: result transport, cancellation propagation, timeout policy, failure envelope, environment inheritance, and recursion guard. Record it in `docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md` under `Q5: delegate contract`.

## Context
This is the handshake between the parent session and the future nested runner in `M4.1`. Without a fixed contract, different delegate tools could drift in timeout behavior, returned shape, or recursion safety.

## Scope
IN:
- define the success envelope and failure envelope returned by delegate tools
- define how parent `AbortSignal` cancellation propagates to the child
- set a default timeout and timeout override policy
- document which env vars/config are inherited by the child
- define how `maxDepth=1` is enforced at runtime
OUT:
- implementation of the runner, prompt porting, or individual delegate tool registration

## Implementation Context
- Future implementation targets likely include `src/subagents/run-nested-pi.ts`, delegate tool modules, and shared result helpers.
- The contract should specify whether nested output is buffered before return or streamed internally then summarized.
- Capture enough TypeScript shape detail that M4 can translate it directly into interfaces/types.

## Constraints
- Default timeout should be explicit (proposal around 5 minutes unless research proves another default is safer).
- Failures must be structured and machine-readable, not free-form only.
- Recursion guard must be runtime-enforced, not prompt-enforced.

## DoD
- Decision section includes:
  - success envelope shape (`content`, `details`, and any metadata)
  - failure envelope shape and error codes/categories
  - cancellation propagation rule
  - default timeout and override story
  - env inheritance allowlist
  - recursion-guard mechanism
  - streaming vs buffered return policy
close_reason: Research: structured success/failure envelopes, 5min default timeout, PI_NESTED_DEPTH recursion guard, buffered return, cancellation via childSession.abort(). Documented in docs/decisions/m0-feasibility.md Q5.

title: M2b: HTTP-backed tools (websearch, context7, grep_app)
description: Umbrella for WS-D. Replace 3 MCP servers with 5 custom HTTP tools using canonical names from PRD §5.1. Each tool needs default timeout, normalized error envelope, secret redaction, explicit retry-or-no-retry policy. See plan §4 WS-D, §8 Slice 2.

title: M2b.0: Shared HTTP client (timeout, error normalization, secret redaction, no-retry default)
description:
## Objective
Implement the shared HTTP client wrapper used by all remote-call tools so timeout handling, normalized errors, and secret redaction behave consistently across websearch, context7, and grep_app integrations.

## Context
This bead centralizes PRD §6 HTTP resilience plus the no-secret-leak requirement. Without it, each HTTP-backed tool would reinvent timeout/error/redaction behavior and drift in unsafe ways.

## Scope
IN:
- `src/tools/_shared/http.ts`
- a thin wrapper around `fetch` (or the project-standard HTTP primitive) with:
  - default timeout
  - `AbortSignal` support
  - normalized success/error result shape
  - explicit no-retry default
  - header/query/body redaction utilities for logs and surfaced errors
- opt-in redaction keys/config for tool-specific secrets
OUT:
- per-tool request/response mapping for individual HTTP tools

## Implementation Context
- Downstream users: all WS-D tool leaves (`websearch_*`, `context7_*`, `grep_app_*`).
- Collaborators: logger from `M1.3`, registration helper patterns from `M2a.0` where useful.
- Prefer a small explicit API such as `httpFetch({ url, method, headers, body, timeoutMs, signal, redactKeys })` returning a discriminated result.

## Constraints
- Default timeout should be explicit (30s is the current working assumption unless a better project default emerges).
- No automatic retries in the shared layer.
- Errors and logs must never contain raw auth headers, raw API keys, or full sensitive query strings.

## DoD
- Unit tests cover timeout, network failure, non-2xx response, and redaction of sensitive headers/query params.
- The helper returns a normalized error shape suitable for direct use by downstream tools.
- Abort/cancellation behavior is documented in the helper contract so later tools can compose it safely.
close_reason: Shared HTTP client with timeout, redaction, normalized errors

title: M2b.1: Implement websearch_search tool (Exa or Tavily based on config)

title: M2b.2: Implement websearch_fetch tool (URL → markdown/text/html)
description:
## Objective
Implement `src/tools/websearch/fetch.ts` registering tool `websearch_fetch` (canonical). Fetches a URL and converts to requested format (`markdown` default).

## Context
This is the follow-up fetch primitive for search-driven workflows. After `websearch_search` surfaces candidate URLs, agents need a deterministic way to retrieve and normalize page content for analysis.

## Scope
IN:

OUT:

- Default format is `markdown`.
- Upgrade `http://` URLs to `https://` before fetch.

- Invalid URL / upstream failure paths return structured, non-crashing errors.

title: M2b.3: Implement context7_resolve_library_id tool
description:
## Objective
Implement `src/tools/context7/resolve.ts` registering tool `context7_resolve_library_id` (canonical). Resolves a library name + query to a Context7-compatible `/org/project` ID.

## Context
This is the discovery half of the Context7 integration. It lets Blackbytes map human-friendly package/library names to the exact `libraryId` required by the docs-query tool.

## Scope
IN:

- Context7 API call through the shared HTTP client

OUT:

## Implementation Context

- Some endpoints may be public, but config may provide `blackbytes.context7.api_key`; support that path cleanly.
- Output should make the selected library ID easy for downstream tools/prompts to consume directly.

## Constraints
- Tool name exact: `context7_resolve_library_id`.
- Params `libraryName` and `query` are both in scope; do not collapse this into a docs-fetch operation.
- Normalize 4xx/5xx errors through the shared HTTP client/error contract.

## DoD

- Tool registers under the canonical name and returns a stable, machine-usable selected-library result.

description:

title: Add SubAgentDeclaration and helper
description:
## Objective
Add `SubAgentDeclaration` and `defineSubAgent()` so builtin and YAML-defined delegates can share one declaration contract without collapsing builtin parameter schemas.

## Context
The current code repeats the same registration pattern across `src/sub-agents/*.ts` and splits metadata across multiple files. The plan makes declarations the new source of truth for prompt text, descriptions, parameter schemas, and tool resolution inputs.

## Scope
In scope: create `src/sub-agents/declaration.ts` (or the chosen equivalent), define the declaration type/helper, and expose any needed shared types through the existing package surfaces. Out of scope: runtime registration, enabled-set changes, YAML file loading, and any runner changes.

## Components Touched
- `src/sub-agents/declaration.ts`
- `src/sub-agents/types.ts`
- any package export surface needed for the public declaration type

## Dependencies / Prerequisites
None; this is the first implementation slice and unblocks the rest of the graph.

## Important Assumptions and Constraints
- Builtin declarations must keep explicit `parameters` and `buildUserPrompt` hooks; the registrar must not assume `{ prompt: string }`.
- Inline prompt text will eventually live with declarations, but prompt migration itself belongs in the builtin migration beads.
- Keep the solution simple-first and aligned with existing ESM/TypeScript patterns.

## Risks / Gotchas
The main risk is designing a declaration shape that is too generic and accidentally erases builtin contract differences, especially `general`'s optional `context` field.

## Validation / Definition of Done
- The new declaration type/helper compiles cleanly.
- Existing builtin modules can import the helper without type suppressions.
- `bun run lint` and `bun run build` pass once the foundation slice is wired.
close_reason: Implemented declaration.ts with SubAgentDeclaration, AllowedToolsResolver, defineSubAgent

title: Add delegable-tool registry and resolver
description:
## Objective
Introduce a conservative delegable-tool registry and resolver that can validate static allowlists/denylists against the nested-session tool surface without overloading `enabledSet.tools`.

## Context
The plan explicitly separates extension-managed enablement from the broader tool names a nested Pi session may receive. This resolves the current mismatch where the code only tracks extension-managed tools but the declaration model needs to support static allow/deny semantics like `all-except-delegates`.

## Scope
In scope: define the explicit registry for extension-managed tools plus curated Pi default tool names, add resolver logic for static allowlist, static denylist, and `all-except-delegates`, and expose validation hooks for declarations. Out of scope: actual delegate registration, startup wiring, and runtime introspection of Pi internals.

## Components Touched
- new or updated shared resolver module under `src/sub-agents/`
- any config/resource metadata glue needed only to source extension-managed tool names
- tests adjacent to the resolver if a small focused unit test is the clearest way to lock semantics

## Dependencies / Prerequisites
Depends on the declaration foundation shape existing so the resolver can target the final declaration contract.

## Important Assumptions and Constraints
- Use an explicit curated registry; do not attempt runtime discovery of Pi default tools.
- Unknown tool names should fail builtin validation and cause YAML declarations to be skipped later with warnings.
- `delegate_*` tools must still be excluded from recursive delegation.

## Risks / Gotchas
It is easy to accidentally blur the meaning of `enabledSet.tools` and the delegable tool surface. Keep them distinct in names, APIs, and tests.

## Validation / Definition of Done
- Resolver outputs the expected tool set for allowlist, denylist, and `all-except-delegates` cases.
- Static validation rejects unknown tool names deterministically.
- `bun run lint` and `bun run build` pass for the resolver slice.
close_reason: Implemented delegable-tools.ts with PI_DEFAULT_TOOLS, DELEGABLE_TOOL_NAMES, validateToolNames, validateBuiltinToolNames, resolveToolStrategy for allowlist/denylist/all-except-delegates. 17 tests.

title: Implement generic sub-agent registrar
description:
## Objective
Implement `registerSubAgent()` so one generic registrar replaces the four bespoke `registerDelegate*Tool()` functions while preserving current nested runner semantics.

## Context
Currently `src/handlers/index.ts` calls four separate functions:
- `registerDelegateExploreTool(pi)` — from `src/sub-agents/explore.ts`
- `registerDelegateGeneralTool(pi)` — from `src/sub-agents/general.ts`
- `registerDelegateLibrarianTool(pi)` — from `src/sub-agents/librarian.ts`
- `registerDelegateOracleTool(pi)` — from `src/sub-agents/oracle.ts`

Each follows the same pattern: check enabledSet, call `pi.registerTool()` with name/description/parameters/execute, read prompt from `src/prompts/*.md`, call `runNestedPi()`. The registrar must generalize this pattern while preserving per-agent differences (parameter schemas, prompt builders, tool resolution).

## Scope
In scope: create `src/sub-agents/register.ts` with `registerSubAgent(pi, declaration, options?)`, connect declaration validation and tool resolution from beads 1.1/1.2, keep compatibility with `runNestedPi()` for prompt composition, model override, reasoning effort, and recursion guard. Out of scope: migrating each builtin module, YAML loading, runner changes.

## Components Touched
- `src/sub-agents/register.ts` (new)
- Declaration/resolver modules from foundation slice
- `src/sub-agents/runner.ts` — consumed but not changed

## Dependencies / Prerequisites
Depends on pib-5td.1.1 (declaration helper) and pib-5td.1.2 (tool resolver).

## Important Assumptions and Constraints
- `runNestedPi()` remains the nested execution path exactly as today.
- Builtin declarations may provide richer parameters and prompt builders than YAML declarations.
- No user-facing tool renames; tool names stay `delegate_<name>`.
- No recursive delegation beyond current depth guard.

## Risks / Gotchas
- A too-opinionated registrar can accidentally normalize builtin behavior. Preserve `oracle` default reasoning behavior and `general`s special prompt composition (`{ prompt, context? }`).
- The registrar must handle both `{ prompt: string }` (simple) and `buildUserPrompt(params)` (rich) patterns.

## Validation / Definition of Done
- Registrar can register a declaration without changing underlying nested runner behavior.
- Type and lint checks pass.
- Ready for builtin modules to migrate onto it in the next slice.

Reads system prompt from declaration path, resolves static/dynamic allowedTools, applies sync/async model overrides via declaration hooks, delegates to runNestedPi(). Updated declaration.ts to support async resolveModelOverrides (ModelOverrides type). Added 11 tests in register.test.ts — all pass.

## Objective
Rewire session startup and prompt augmentation to assemble declarations before initialization, register enabled delegates in a loop, and advertise only enabled runtime metadata.

## Context
The plan identifies startup ordering as a major risk: builtin and YAML declarations must be known before enablement is finalized, and `before-agent-start` must read runtime metadata rather than static builtin constants.

## Scope

## Dependencies / Prerequisites
Depends on builtin declarations, runtime metadata registry, and dynamic enabled-set/schema support. The final startup composition bead will later extend this with YAML-loaded declarations.

## Important Assumptions and Constraints
- Registration should skip disabled agents cleanly.

title: Implement YAML declaration loader
description:
## Objective
Add the YAML dependency and implement a user declaration loader that reads `$PI_AGENT_DIR/sub-agents/*.yaml`, validates files independently, and returns only valid declarations.

## Context
User-defined YAML agents are the new extensibility surface, but they are untrusted disk input. The plan requires per-file error isolation, home-directory fallback behavior consistent with config loading, and a constrained declaration contract for user agents.

## Scope
In scope: add the `yaml` dependency, define the user declaration schema, resolve the sub-agent directory under `PI_AGENT_DIR`/`~/.pi/agent`, parse `*.yaml` files, and log warnings for syntax/schema/tool-validation failures while skipping only the bad file. Out of scope: duplicate-name enforcement across the full declaration set and final startup assembly.

## Components Touched
- `package.json` / lockfile for the `yaml` dependency
- `src/sub-agents/loader.ts`
- user declaration schema/types under `src/sub-agents/`
- logging hooks used during startup warnings

## Dependencies / Prerequisites
Depends on the declaration model and delegable-tool resolver semantics so YAML declarations can validate against the same contract and tool registry.

## Important Assumptions and Constraints
- YAML agents are intentionally limited to the default `{ prompt: string }` contract and static allow/deny settings.
- Unknown tool names in YAML should be treated as invalid-file warnings, not runtime passthrough.
- No `.ts` / `.js` user agent format and no hot reload.

## Risks / Gotchas
Directory resolution and partial-default behavior are easy places to get wrong. Fail closed on malformed declarations and avoid silently inventing defaults beyond the schema.

## Validation / Definition of Done
- Valid YAML declarations load from the documented directory.
- Invalid syntax or schema issues emit warnings and skip only the offending file.
- Build passes with the new dependency and loader module.
close_reason: Implemented YAML declaration loader

title: Compose startup declarations and reject duplicates
description:
## Objective
Assert unique sub-agent names across builtin and YAML declarations, then wire startup composition so combined declarations are validated before enablement and registration begin.

## Context
The plan intentionally treats duplicate names as session initialization errors because they create ambiguous tool identity and metadata ownership. This startup composition step is what turns the loader and dynamic metadata work into a coherent session-start flow.

## Scope
In scope: add the uniqueness assertion helper, combine builtin + user declarations before `initEnabledSet()`, abort session initialization on duplicate names, and ensure metadata/registration only proceed after the declaration set is validated. Out of scope: changing the bootstrap failure boundary or softening duplicates to warning-only behavior.

## Components Touched
- startup composition in `src/handlers/index.ts`
- duplicate validation helper under `src/sub-agents/`
- metadata/enablement integration points that consume the final declaration set

## Dependencies / Prerequisites
Depends on YAML loading, runtime metadata plumbing, and dynamic enabled-set/startup wiring.

## Important Assumptions and Constraints
- Invalid YAML remains recoverable skip-and-warn behavior; duplicate names are not recoverable within the session-start path.
- The extension process survives because bootstrap already wraps the handler failure boundary.
- Do not degrade silently to builtin-only registration when duplicates exist.

## Risks / Gotchas
The biggest risk is ordering: computing enablement before the final declaration set is assembled will make custom agents invisible to both registration and prompt advertisement.

## Validation / Definition of Done
- Duplicate names fail session initialization deterministically before registration.
- Valid builtin + YAML declarations participate in enablement and metadata registration.
- Session-start integration behavior matches the plan's failure boundary.

## Components Touched
- new loader-focused tests under `src/sub-agents/__tests__/`
- `src/__tests__/integration/session-start.test.ts`
- temp-directory test helpers used for `PI_AGENT_DIR` scenarios

## Dependencies / Prerequisites
Depends on the YAML loader and final startup composition beads.

## Important Assumptions and Constraints
- Follow the same temp-directory pattern already used by config-related integration tests.
- Duplicate-name failure is intentional and should remain a session-start error.
- Invalid YAML files are skipped with warnings rather than blocking the entire startup path.

## Risks / Gotchas
These tests should verify the order of operations implicitly by asserting behavior at session start; otherwise sequencing regressions can slip through.

## Validation / Definition of Done
- End-to-end tests cover the major YAML success and failure modes.
- Session-start integration tests demonstrate that valid user agents are advertised/registered and disabled ones are filtered out.
- Full test suite is ready for the repo verification gate.

description:

title: Polish exports and docs after implementation
description:
## Objective
Finish export/doc polish after the declaration system lands so package consumers and repo docs reflect the new runtime model without expanding implementation scope.

## Context
The plan reserves a post-MVP pass for exported types and docs touchups once the risky implementation work is done. This bead makes the shipped surface coherent without reopening core design decisions.

## Scope
In scope: review any package exports for declaration-related types/helpers, clean up docs or references that still describe static builtin-only metadata, and confirm prompt copy plus implementation notes still state that `temperature` remains config-only and `runNestedPi()` stays unchanged. Out of scope: new commands, new extensibility formats, or further architecture changes.

## Components Touched

title: Subagent hardening and delegation quality roadmap
description:
## Success Criteria
- Phase 1 hardening beads make sub-agent tool allowlists, config/runtime behavior, failure diagnostics, YAML/status diagnostics, and session reset consistent and verified.
- Phase 2 quality beads add timeout, controlled prompt inheritance, conservative fallback, and optional progress plumbing without introducing background agents, parallel fanout, worktree isolation, persistent memory, public delegate renames, or in-process runner replacement.
- All leaf beads remain self-contained for implementation without reading docs/plans/subagent-hardening-and-quality-plan.md.

## Scope
This epic tracks the reviewed Subagent Hardening and Quality Improvements plan for pi-blackbytes. It preserves subprocess-based nested Pi execution, no recursive delegation, conservative env forwarding, and minimal dependencies.

title: Phase 1 subagent hardening and correctness
description:
## Acceptance Criteria
- Nested CLI capabilities are confirmed before code widens built-in tool allowlists or passes new CLI flags.
- Tool resolution, temperature behavior, per-agent config resolution, failure diagnostics, session reset, and YAML/status diagnostics are implemented with regression tests.
- Full verification passes with `bun run lint`, `bun run build`, and `bun run test`.

## Scope
Hardens existing declaration-driven sub-agents without adding fallback chains, prompt inheritance, streaming/progress UX, background agents, parallel delegation, or worktree isolation.

title: Confirm nested Pi CLI capabilities and cwd contract
description:
## Objective
Produce deterministic compatibility evidence for nested `pi -p` execution before any implementation widens tool allowlists or passes new CLI flags.

## Context
The current sub-agent runner assumes `--tools`, `--model`, and `--thinking` work, while the plan needs confirmed Pi built-in tool names, possible `--temperature` support, and a safe working-directory contract for write-capable `general` delegation.

## Scope
In scope: inspect local Pi CLI help and/or package source for accepted nested flags and built-in tool names; determine unknown-tool behavior; verify whether `--temperature` is accepted; define how `runNestedPi()` chooses cwd, preferring active workspace/repo root from host API and otherwise documenting `process.cwd()` fallback; encode decisions in deterministic tests/fixtures. Out of scope: requiring live provider auth, network, model availability, or real nested `pi -p` runs in default CI.

## Components Touched
- `src/sub-agents/runner.ts` generated args/spawn options tests
- `src/sub-agents/register.ts` tests if cwd/flags are passed from registration
- compatibility note or fixture under docs/test fixtures if behavior cannot be unit-tested
- package/source/help inspection fixtures as needed

## Dependencies / Prerequisites
None. This bead is the prerequisite for tool-surface and temperature implementation beads.

## Important Assumptions and Constraints
- Default tests must be deterministic and must not require an installed `pi` binary, provider authentication, network, or model availability.
- Real authenticated nested `pi -p` smoke runs are optional local evidence only unless an explicit integration profile is added.
- Do not add built-in tool names or pass `--temperature` downstream until this bead records concrete supported/unsupported decisions.
- Any bead that launches nested Pi must carry and test the cwd contract.

## Risks / Gotchas
Pi CLI/package behavior can drift; fixtures or generated-argument tests should fail loudly when accepted flags/tool names change.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Evidence states Pi CLI/package version inspected, supported flags, accepted built-in tool names for `--tools`, unknown-tool behavior, `--temperature` support status, and cwd contract.
- Generated-argument/spawn-option tests cover nested `--tools` args and cwd without live Pi/auth/network/model requirements.
- Downstream plan branches about built-in tool names and temperature are resolved into concrete instructions for implementers.
close_reason: Implemented: recorded Pi CLI compatibility evidence and covered nested tool/cwd behavior with deterministic tests

title: Add canonical delegable tool registry and nested-tool finalizer
description:
## Objective
Create the canonical nested-tool registry and central finalization step that every sub-agent path uses before calling `runNestedPi()`.

## Context
`enabledSet.tools` only tracks extension-managed tools, while nested `--tools` also needs confirmed Pi built-ins. Recursive delegate tools and globally disabled tools must never bypass resolver branches.

## Scope
In scope: define explicit tool classes for extension tools, confirmed Pi built-ins, delegable tools, safe read/search/docs tools, and mutating/exec tools; source canonical public names from `src/config/resource-metadata.ts` and `src/sub-agents/delegable-tools.ts`; implement a finalizer that deduplicates, rejects unknown names, removes/rejects `delegate_*`, applies global `blackbytes.disabled_tools`, enforces per-agent policy, and returns deterministic ordering. Out of scope: public tool renames, aliases such as `ast_grep_search`/`ast_grep_replace`, or granting YAML defaults write/exec tools.

## Components Touched
- `src/sub-agents/delegable-tools.ts`
- `src/config/resource-metadata.ts` if canonical metadata needs adjustment
- resolver/register tests under `src/sub-agents/__tests__/`

## Dependencies / Prerequisites
Depends on the CLI compatibility bead resolving supported built-in tool names.

## Important Assumptions and Constraints
- `blackbytes.disabled_tools` is a global denylist for every nested allowlist path, including extension tools and confirmed Pi built-ins where feasible.
- `delegate_*` is invalid in all static, dynamic, and YAML allowlists and must be absent from generated `--tools`.
- If Pi built-ins cannot be disabled directly, status must later explain that disabling `bash`/`write`/`edit` requires disabling write-capable agents.

## Risks / Gotchas
Do not let resolver-specific code pass user-supplied tool names directly to the runner; all paths must converge through the finalizer.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests prove unknown names and `delegate_*` names are rejected or excluded before generated args are built.
- Unit tests prove globally disabled tools cannot reach `runNestedPi()` through builtin static lists, dynamic broad lists, YAML default/denylist mode, or YAML explicit allowlists.
- Generated `--tools` tests use canonical public tool names and contain no unknown aliases.

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [POLISH] When implementing the canonical registry, make the mutability classification explicit and test-backed rather than inferred ad hoc: read/search/docs examples should include read, grep, glob, ast_grep_search, websearch_*, context7_*, and grep_app_*; mutating/exec examples should include write/edit/bash, hashline_edit, and ast_grep_replace. Keep this classification close to the finalizer so Phase 2 fallback can reuse the same resolved allowlist mutability instead of duplicating agent-name checks.

title: Fix builtin and YAML tool resolvers with general safety overlay
description:
## Objective
Update sub-agent allowed-tool resolution so builtins and YAML agents receive the intended capability surface, with `general` broad access paired with a bounded safety/context overlay.

## Context
`general` currently advertises read/write/edit/bash but broad resolution only sees extension tools, while YAML defaults must remain conservative and must not inherit `general` write/exec access.

## Scope
In scope: split `general` broad resolution from YAML default/denylist resolution; keep `explore`, `oracle`, and `librarian` read-only/research-only; make YAML omitted `allowed_tools` default to safe read/search/docs tools; allow YAML explicit mutating/exec tools only when valid and not globally disabled; reject/skip YAML explicit disabled-tool requests with diagnostics; add or prepend a bounded `general` execution-safety overlay. Out of scope: Phase 2 prompt inheritance, recursive delegation, public delegate renames, or worktree isolation.

## Components Touched
- `src/sub-agents/general.ts`
- `src/sub-agents/declaration.ts` if resolver types change
- `src/sub-agents/register.ts`
- `src/sub-agents/loader.ts`
- `src/sub-agents/__tests__/delegable-tools.test.ts`, `delegates.test.ts`, `loader.test.ts` or focused equivalents

## Dependencies / Prerequisites
Depends on the compatibility bead and the canonical nested-tool finalizer bead.

## Important Assumptions and Constraints
- The `general` safety/context overlay source contract must be concrete before broad tools are enabled: exact files/session fields, precedence, max size, redaction policy, and fallback behavior when repo instructions are unavailable.
- The overlay is Phase 1 safety context and is separate from Phase 2 prompt inheritance.
- YAML `denied_tools` applies to the conservative YAML base, not to `general` broad access.
- Working-directory contract from the compatibility bead must be reflected in generated spawn-option tests for write-capable `general`.

## Risks / Gotchas
Widening defaults incorrectly could grant custom YAML agents `write`, `edit`, `bash`, `hashline_edit`, or `ast_replace` without explicit opt-in.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests prove `delegate_general` generated `--tools` includes confirmed Pi built-ins plus enabled extension tools, excludes `delegate_*`, and respects global disabled tools.
- Tests prove default YAML and YAML denylist modes exclude mutating/exec tools by default.
- Tests prove YAML explicit allowlists can include confirmed mutating/exec tools only when valid and not disabled; disabled explicit requests are skipped/rejected with visible diagnostics.
- Tests/manual smoke prove broad `general` access is paired with the safety overlay and repo constraints are visible enough.

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [POLISH] Clarify the Phase 1 general safety/context overlay before enabling broad mutating tools: build it from already-loaded repo/session instruction sources rather than ad hoc unbounded reads; include current working directory/workspace root, enabled/disabled resources, no-recursive-delegation, git/secret safety, and AGENTS.md-derived repo constraints when available; cap the block to a deterministic small size (roughly 8-12KB), redact obvious secrets/config values, and fall back to a minimal built-in safety block if repo instructions cannot be read. Keep this overlay separate from Phase 2 promptMode append inheritance and test that delegate_general receives it whenever broad tools are passed.

title: Resolve temperature config drift end-to-end
description:
## Objective
Make the existing `temperature` configuration either real runtime behavior or visibly unsupported/reserved, eliminating silent no-op behavior.

## Context
`temperature` is accepted in config today but is not passed through `ModelOverrides` or runner CLI args. Passing unsupported Pi CLI flags would break nested delegation.

## Scope
In scope: use the compatibility bead decision for `--temperature`; if supported, add `temperature` to model overrides, JSON/YAML config input, resolver path, runner args, setup-models/status/docs, and tests; if unsupported, preserve/parse existing nested config safely and surface it as reserved/unsupported in status/docs/tests without passing the flag. Out of scope: fallback model chains and timeout fields, which are later config expansions.

## Components Touched
- `src/config/schema.ts`
- `src/sub-agents/declaration.ts`
- `src/sub-agents/register.ts`
- `src/sub-agents/runner.ts`
- `src/sub-agents/loader.ts`
- `src/commands/setup-models.ts` if wizard output/docs mention temperature
- related config/sub-agent/command tests

## Dependencies / Prerequisites
Depends on the CLI compatibility bead.

## Important Assumptions and Constraints
- Do not pass `--temperature` unless the compatibility evidence confirms support.
- Prefer transparent unsupported/reserved status over silent no-op.
- Preserve unknown nested agent config fields with passthrough before removing or relabeling active fields.

## Risks / Gotchas
Nested `sub_agents.<name>` schema may not currently preserve unknown fields; avoid breaking existing user settings.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Schema/register/runner/setup-models tests match the concrete supported-or-unsupported temperature decision.
- Status or docs clearly communicate unsupported/reserved temperature if the CLI does not support it.
- No unsupported CLI flag is generated for nested Pi.

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [POLISH] Make the supported-vs-reserved temperature branch visible in both code and tests. If unsupported, continue accepting existing JSON/YAML temperature fields without throwing, do not emit --temperature, show reserved/unsupported in /blackbytes-status, and avoid prompting for it in /setup-models except as documentation. If supported, add one generated-args test proving the exact CLI flag/value format from the compatibility bead.

title: Centralize per-agent config resolution and session snapshot
description:
## Objective
Add one deterministic per-agent config resolver and capture resolved sub-agent runtime settings in a session-scoped snapshot used by both delegate execution and `/blackbytes-status`.

## Context
Model/reasoning/temperature behavior must not drift across declarations, YAML, JSON settings, delegate execution, and status. Disk config or YAML changes after startup should not silently affect an active host session.

## Scope
In scope: implement a resolver with precedence `declaration defaults < YAML declaration fields < JSON blackbytes.sub_agents.<name> overrides`; preserve unknown nested fields with `.passthrough()`; route builtins, YAML declarations, `registerSubAgent()`, and status through the resolved snapshot; add tests for builtins, YAML agents, absent config, invalid values, unknown field preservation, precedence, and post-startup disk changes. Out of scope: adding fallback or timeout fields beyond hooks needed for later reuse.

## Components Touched
- `src/config/schema.ts`
- `src/sub-agents/declaration.ts`
- `src/sub-agents/register.ts`
- `src/sub-agents/loader.ts`
- `src/handlers/index.ts`
- `/blackbytes-status` command implementation under `src/commands/`
- config/sub-agent/command tests

## Dependencies / Prerequisites
Depends on the temperature-decision bead so resolver semantics include the concrete temperature behavior.

## Important Assumptions and Constraints
- This snapshot is about Blackbytes extension config lifecycle; nested Pi still runs with `--no-session --no-context-files`.
- Delegate execution and `/blackbytes-status` must read from the same active-session snapshot.
- Disk config/YAML changes after startup are pending next session unless a future reload command exists.

## Risks / Gotchas
Avoid reloading `settings.json` during delegate execution or status in ways that disagree with registered declarations.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Resolver tests prove precedence, invalid-value handling, unknown field preservation, and builtin/YAML behavior.
- Tests prove after `handleSessionStart()`, status and the next delegate invocation remain consistent with the active snapshot even if disk files change.
- Register tests prove model/reasoning/temperature-reserved behavior comes from the central resolver.

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [POLISH] Make the active-session snapshot concrete enough for later beads to reuse: store resolved per-agent summaries keyed by agent name, declaration source (builtin or YAML path), resolved model/reasoning/temperature-reserved semantics, resolved timeout placeholder if introduced later, final tool allowlist summary, disabled/skipped reasons, and YAML diagnostics references. Delegate execution and /blackbytes-status should consume this immutable in-memory snapshot; tests should mutate settings/YAML after startup and prove both status and the next delegate call continue using the old snapshot until the next session_start.

title: Improve nested failure formatting, bounded output, and cancellation
description:
## Objective
Return actionable, safe delegate failure details while bounding child output, classifying failures, and handling timeout/cancellation robustly.

## Context
`DelegateResult.details` exists but is not surfaced in registered tool results, making nested subprocess failures hard to debug. Fallback later needs structured failure classification but must not parse redacted/truncated user-visible text.

## Scope
In scope: add a safe formatter/truncator for failure output; redact obvious secrets and environment dumps; preserve deterministic head/tail truncation around 4-8 KB; bound runner stdout/stderr collection before formatting; escalate timeout/cancel from SIGTERM to SIGKILL after a short grace period; discover whether Pi tool execution exposes an abort/cancel signal and wire it from `registerSubAgent()` to `runNestedPi()` if available, otherwise document timeout-only cancellation; add structured failure classification such as failed, timed_out, cancelled, spawn_error, recursion_refused, and distinguishable CLI/tool-configuration errors. Out of scope: model fallback execution.

## Components Touched
- `src/sub-agents/register.ts`
- `src/sub-agents/runner.ts`
- `src/sub-agents/types.ts` if result types change
- `src/sub-agents/__tests__/register.test.ts`
- `src/sub-agents/__tests__/runner.test.ts`

## Dependencies / Prerequisites
Can start independently, but coordinate with tool-resolution test changes if both touch register/runner seams.

## Important Assumptions and Constraints
- Final tool result shape remains `{ content: [{ type: "text", text }] }`.
- Internal raw stderr/error data may be bounded and used only for structured classification; user-visible results, logs, and status must use redacted/truncated details.
- Do not retry or implement fallback here; only provide classification and details needed by later fallback.
- If host cancellation cannot be wired, document that nested cancellation is timeout-based only.

## Risks / Gotchas
Bounding only final display text is insufficient; noisy child processes must not accumulate unbounded memory internally.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runner/register tests cover stderr details, spawn errors, deterministic truncation, redaction, high-output children, timeouts, cancellation, SIGKILL escalation, and deterministic failure classification.
- Tests prove timeout/cancel resolves within `timeoutMs + graceMs` for children that ignore SIGTERM.
- Host-tool-call cancellation propagation is tested when Pi API exposes a signal, or unsupported behavior is documented.
close_reason: Implemented: added structured delegate failure classification, redacted/truncated diagnostics, bounded child output, and SIGKILL escalation

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [POLISH] Keep two separate failure surfaces: an internal structured result for retry/fallback decisions and a user-visible redacted/truncated text formatter. Suggested classification values are success, failed, timed_out, cancelled, spawn_error, recursion_refused, cli_usage_error, invalid_tool_allowlist, and provider_or_model_unavailable. Never base future fallback on display text. Bound stdout/stderr while collecting from the child, not only when rendering; tests should include a child that emits more than the bound before exiting.

title: Reset session-scoped runtime state at startup
description:
## Objective
Make repeated `handleSessionStart()` runs idempotent by clearing session-scoped singleton state before registering resources.

## Context
Current state such as enabled-set initialization, runtime sub-agent metadata, and YAML diagnostics can leak or duplicate across startup runs, causing false duplicate errors or stale status output.

## Scope
In scope: add a production-intent reset helper such as `resetSessionRuntimeState()`; reset enabled-set, runtime sub-agent metadata registry, YAML diagnostics, and any session-derived prompt/resource metadata before loading YAML or registering builtins; add repeated-startup and failed-then-successful-startup tests. Out of scope: using test-only reset helpers directly from production code unless promoted/renamed.

## Components Touched
- `src/handlers/index.ts`
- `src/config/enabled-set.ts`
- `src/config/resource-metadata.ts`
- YAML diagnostics module once created, or a placeholder reset hook
- startup/session tests

## Dependencies / Prerequisites
None, but this bead must complete before YAML/status diagnostics rely on clean session state.

## Important Assumptions and Constraints
- Reset must be the first session-runtime mutation in `handleSessionStart()`, before YAML loading or builtin registration, so failed startups cannot leave mixed old/new metadata.
- Runtime state reset should be safe for production startup, not just tests.

## Risks / Gotchas
Do not accidentally erase persistent user config; only clear in-memory session/runtime registries.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests calling `handleSessionStart()` twice in one process do not get duplicate metadata or enabled-set initialization errors.
- Tests prove diagnostics/metadata from startup 1 do not appear after startup 2 with different files/config.
- Failed-startup-then-successful-startup test proves partial initialization does not poison the next session.

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [POLISH] The reset helper should be production code invoked at the very top of handleSessionStart(), before config-derived enablement, YAML loading, or builtin registration. Include a failed-startup fixture that partially registers runtime metadata/diagnostics, then verify a second successful startup has only the new session state. Do not reset persistent settings or JSONL/bead state.

title: Add YAML diagnostics and safe active-session status output
description:
## Objective
Make user-defined sub-agent loading transparent in `/blackbytes-status` using diagnostics and resolved config from the active session snapshot.

## Context
YAML warnings are currently logger-only, so users may not know why custom agents are missing. Status must reflect declarations actually registered at startup and must not expose prompts, secrets, raw stderr, or stale disk changes.

## Scope
In scope: collect YAML diagnostics for directory, scanned files, loaded declarations, skipped files, and errors; enforce duplicate policy where builtin duplicates remain invariant failures but YAML duplicates are skipped with diagnostics; pass builtin names as reserved names and track accepted YAML names in sorted file order; update `/blackbytes-status` with builtin agents, loaded YAML agents, skipped YAML files/reasons, disabled agents, unsupported/reserved config fields, resolved per-agent summaries, and allowlist summaries; label post-startup disk changes as pending-next-session if mentioned. Out of scope: adding JSON mode unless command framework already supports it with minimal changes.

## Components Touched
- `src/sub-agents/loader.ts`
- new `src/sub-agents/diagnostics.ts` or similar
- `src/config/resource-metadata.ts`
- `/blackbytes-status` command under `src/commands/`
- loader/status/startup tests

## Dependencies / Prerequisites
Depends on session runtime reset, tool-surface final allowlists, temperature behavior, and central config/session snapshot.

## Important Assumptions and Constraints
- Status must read the active session snapshot captured at startup, not reload YAML/config from disk.
- Do not show `system_prompt`, full YAML contents, raw nested stderr, secrets, or unbounded config objects.
- Duplicate YAML diagnostic must include skipped file, duplicate name, and winning source (`builtin` or earlier YAML file).
- Allowlist summaries can show complete lists only when short; otherwise show counts/categorized names.

## Risks / Gotchas
Do not call `assertUniqueNames([...builtins, ...yaml])` in a way that crashes startup on user YAML duplicates.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Loader/status tests cover valid YAML, invalid syntax, unknown tool names, duplicate-with-builtin, duplicate-between-YAML-files, disabled custom agents, and missing YAML dir.
- Tests prove duplicate YAML files are skipped with visible diagnostics while non-conflicting agents still register; duplicate builtin declarations still fail clearly.
- Status tests prove output reflects active-session snapshot even if YAML/config files change after startup.
- A paired delegate-execution test proves values passed to `runNestedPi()` match status snapshot.

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [POLISH] For status output, prefer a concise table/sections over dumping raw objects: builtin agents, loaded YAML agents, skipped YAML files with reason, disabled agents/tools, resolved model/reasoning/temperature/timeout fields, and allowlist counts/categories. Include full allowlists only when short and safe. Do not display system_prompt, raw YAML bodies, raw nested stderr, API keys, env vars, or unbounded config values. Duplicate YAML diagnostics should name the skipped file, duplicate agent name, and winning source.

title: Document and verify Phase 1 subagent hardening
description:
## Objective
Close Phase 1 by documenting hardened behavior and running the repository verification gates.

## Context
Phase 1 changes touch security-sensitive delegation, config behavior, diagnostics, and status. Users and future implementers need current docs and passing verification before Phase 2 expands behavior.

## Scope
In scope: update README/AGENTS/config examples/YAML examples as needed for tool allowlists, disabled tools, temperature behavior, session snapshot semantics, failure details, and status diagnostics; run `bun run lint`, `bun run build`, and `bun run test`; optionally run `bun run check:size` if code grew materially; capture any local-only Pi smoke evidence as optional documentation. Out of scope: Phase 2 timeout, prompt inheritance, fallback, or progress implementation.

## Components Touched
- README/config docs/YAML examples as applicable
- `AGENTS.md` only if project instructions or command docs changed
- optional compatibility notes from Phase 1

## Dependencies / Prerequisites
Depends on Phase 1 implementation beads for tool surface, temperature/config resolver, failure diagnostics, session reset, and YAML/status diagnostics.

## Important Assumptions and Constraints
- Verification order from AGENTS.md is `bun run lint`, `bun run build`, `bun run test`.
- Do not add new dependencies unless explicitly justified and approved.

## Risks / Gotchas
Docs must not promise unsupported `--temperature`, progress streaming, fallback, or append-mode behavior before Phase 2 implements them.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Documentation reflects actual Phase 1 behavior and security constraints.
- `bun run lint`, `bun run build`, and `bun run test` pass.
- `bun run check:size` is run or explicitly deemed unnecessary if package size impact is negligible.

title: Phase 2 delegation quality and reliability
description:
## Acceptance Criteria
- Per-agent timeout is configurable and passed to nested runner.
- Prompt builder supports static/append mode with static as default and append enabled only after a safe inherited-context source contract exists.
- Conservative model fallback works for read-only agents only and shares a total timeout budget.
- Optional progress/streaming spike either wires a safe host progress surface or documents unsupported behavior.
- Full verification passes after Phase 2 changes.

## Scope
Improves delegation reliability and prompt quality while preserving subprocess execution, no recursive delegation, no background task lifecycle, no parallel fanout, no worktree isolation, and no persistent memory.

title: Add per-agent timeout configuration and runner pass-through
description:
## Objective
Make nested sub-agent execution duration explicit and configurable per agent.

## Context
The runner has a timeout behavior, but Phase 2 needs per-agent defaults and config/YAML overrides before model fallback can share one total-chain budget.

## Scope
In scope: extend declaration defaults, JSON `blackbytes.sub_agents.<name>` config, YAML schema, and central resolver with `timeoutMs`/`timeout_ms`; set builtin defaults (`explore` 120000, `librarian` 240000, `oracle` 300000, `general` 600000); pass resolved timeout from `registerSubAgent()` to `runNestedPi()`; add tests for precedence, runner args/options, and timeout behavior. Out of scope: model fallback logic and progress streaming.

## Components Touched
- `src/config/schema.ts`
- `src/sub-agents/declaration.ts`
- `src/sub-agents/register.ts`
- `src/sub-agents/runner.ts`
- builtin declarations under `src/sub-agents/`
- config/register/runner/loader tests

## Dependencies / Prerequisites
Depends on Phase 1 config cleanup and central per-agent resolver/session snapshot.

## Important Assumptions and Constraints
- Use bounded defaults; do not leave long-running nested calls unbounded.
- Preserve existing runner timeout behavior except for taking resolved per-agent timeout values.
- Timeout values shown by status and used by delegate execution must come from the same active-session snapshot.

## Risks / Gotchas
Schema changes share files with fallback; do not implement fallback fields in this bead beyond compatibility hooks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests prove builtin default timeouts, YAML/JSON override precedence, and pass-through to `runNestedPi()`.
- Runner tests still prove timeout enforcement and cancellation/SIGKILL behavior.
- Status/delegate snapshot tests include timeout when status has been updated to display it.

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [POLISH] Specify timeout validation and naming in the resolver: JSON settings use timeoutMs, YAML uses timeout_ms, resolved snapshot/status may display timeoutMs consistently. Reject or diagnose non-positive, non-integer, or unreasonably huge values using the same invalid-value behavior as model/reasoning config. Runner tests should assert the resolved timeout is passed as an option to runNestedPi(), not a nested Pi CLI flag.

title: Add promptMode schema and safe prompt builder
description:
## Objective
Implement prompt-building infrastructure for `static` and opt-in `append` prompt modes without changing existing builtin behavior.

## Context
Selected subagents may benefit from parent/session instructions, but inherited context can leak secrets, create prompt bloat, or conflict with sub-agent boundaries. Existing static prompts must remain stable until append mode is explicitly enabled.

## Scope
In scope: add `promptMode?: "static" | "append"` to declarations and YAML schema; implement a prompt builder that preserves static output exactly and wraps append context with an explicit inherited-instructions block, sub-agent boundary, no-recursive-delegation instruction, and sub-agent system prompt; determine and document the inherited-context source contract including source module/API, max size, redaction policy, refresh timing, and confirmation that hidden prompts, raw transcripts, and tool outputs are excluded. If no safe source exists, implement static builder support and document append as unsupported/deferred. Out of scope: enabling append mode for builtins and fallback logic.

## Components Touched
- `src/sub-agents/declaration.ts`
- new `src/sub-agents/prompt-builder.ts`
- `src/sub-agents/register.ts`
- `src/sub-agents/loader.ts`
- prompt-builder/register/loader tests

## Dependencies / Prerequisites
Depends on Phase 1 diagnostics/config stability. The inherited-context source contract must be concrete before append behavior is implemented or enabled.

## Important Assumptions and Constraints
- Default for existing agents is `static`; static mode must preserve current prompt text exactly.
- Child/sub-agent constraints override inherited parent instructions on conflict.
- The builder must not include hidden prompts, raw runtime transcripts, tool outputs, secrets, or unbounded context.
- Nested agents still must not receive delegate tools.

## Risks / Gotchas
Do not fabricate inherited context if Pi execution context does not expose a safe source; defer append rather than adding unsafe ad hoc file reads.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Snapshot/structured tests prove static output is unchanged.
- Append-output tests exist only after source contract is concrete and prove deterministic wrapping, size bounds, redaction, and conflict boundary.
- YAML schema tests cover valid/invalid `promptMode` values.

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [POLISH] Treat append prompt support as source-contract-first. The implementation should first identify a safe parent/session instruction source exposed by Pi; if none exists, still land promptMode schema plus a static prompt builder and document append as unsupported/deferred rather than reading arbitrary files or transcript content. Static mode must be byte-for-byte stable in snapshot tests. Append tests, if enabled, must prove deterministic ordering, conflict boundary text, size cap, and redaction.

title: Enable append prompt mode selectively after validation
description:
## Objective
Opt selected builtins into append prompt mode only after the prompt builder and inherited-context source are validated.

## Context
Append mode can improve context for `oracle` and possibly `general`, but `general` can write files and must preserve stricter execution boundaries. The plan requires default static behavior until manual and automated validation pass.

## Scope
In scope: enable append mode for `oracle` first after Slice 2.2 tests/manual smoke pass; evaluate `general` separately and only enable if the safety/context boundary remains clear for write-capable execution; update prompt snapshots and manual smoke notes. Out of scope: enabling append for all agents, changing delegate public parameter shapes, or introducing persistent memory.

## Components Touched
- builtin declarations under `src/sub-agents/`
- prompt/register tests
- docs or smoke-test notes if append is enabled

## Dependencies / Prerequisites
Depends on the prompt builder bead and concrete inherited-context source contract.

## Important Assumptions and Constraints
- Static remains the default for any agent not explicitly validated.
- Child constraints and no-recursive-delegation boundary override inherited instructions.
- `general` requires extra caution because it has mutating/exec tools.

## Risks / Gotchas
Prompt bloat or conflicting instructions can reduce sub-agent reliability; keep manual validation explicit.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `oracle` append enablement is covered by prompt tests and manual nested-call validation, or the bead documents why enablement remains deferred.
- `general` is either separately validated with write-safety boundaries or left static with a documented reason.
- No builtin silently switches to append without tests proving deterministic prompt shape.

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [POLISH] This bead can complete with an explicit deferred decision if append mode is not safe to enable after Slice 2.2. In that case, leave all builtins static, add docs/smoke notes explaining the source-contract gap, and update tests to prove no builtin changed prompt mode accidentally. Do not force append enablement just to satisfy the phase checklist.

title: Implement conservative model fallback for read-only agents
description:
## Objective
Add model fallback chains for read-only subagents under conservative retry rules and one total timeout budget.

## Context
Fallback improves reliability when a preferred model is unavailable or rate-limited, but retrying write-capable agents can duplicate side effects. Fallback must consume structured failure classification from Phase 1 instead of reparsing redacted user-visible details.

## Scope
In scope: add additive config fields `fallbackModels?: string[]` in JSON config and `fallback_models` in YAML; extend `ModelOverrides` and the central resolver; implement fallback executor around `runNestedPi()` or register path; enable fallback only for read-only agents (`explore`, `oracle`, `librarian`) and reject/disable it for any resolved allowlist containing mutating/exec tools; retry only recognized provider/model availability failures such as rate limit, unavailable model, provider rejection, or transient provider errors; include attempted model details in failure output. Out of scope: fallback for `general` or any mutating YAML agent, worktree isolation, or side-effect detection.

## Components Touched
- `src/config/schema.ts`
- `src/sub-agents/declaration.ts`
- `src/sub-agents/register.ts`
- `src/sub-agents/runner.ts` or new `src/sub-agents/fallback.ts`
- `src/sub-agents/loader.ts`
- builtin declarations/config/status docs/tests as needed

## Dependencies / Prerequisites
Depends on Phase 1 failure classification/formatting, central config resolver, and per-agent timeout support.

## Important Assumptions and Constraints
- Preserve existing `model: string`; do not reinterpret model arrays.
- Fallback must not retry timeout, cancellation, recursion refusal, invalid CLI flags, invalid tool allowlists, or any attempt that produced stdout.
- Fallback attempts share one total-chain timeout budget by default, calculating remaining time for each attempt.
- Fallback eligibility must be verified against resolved tool allowlist, including YAML explicit tools.

## Risks / Gotchas
Do not parse redacted/truncated details for retry decisions; use structured internal failure classification.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests cover first-model success, fallback success, all models fail with attemptedModels, no model override, no retry for timeout/cancel/recursion/invalid flags/invalid allowlist/stdout-before-failure, and fallback configured on mutating/exec agents.
- Tests prove read-only agents can use fallback and `general`/mutating YAML agents cannot.
- Tests prove total-chain timeout budget is shared across attempts.

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [POLISH] Fallback retry eligibility should be computed from internal failure classification and resolved allowlist mutability, not from agent name alone. A YAML agent with any write/edit/bash/hashline_edit/ast_grep_replace-style mutating tool is ineligible even if its prompt is read-only. Do not retry attempts that produced stdout, timed out, were cancelled, hit recursion refusal, failed CLI/tool validation, or may have caused side effects. Attempt summaries shown to users should list model names/statuses but keep stderr redacted/truncated by the Phase 1 formatter.

title: Spike safe progress or streaming surface for nested agents
description:
## Objective
Determine whether existing `runNestedPi()` `onUpdate` output can be connected to a safe host progress/log surface without polluting final tool results or parent context.

## Context
The runner has internal `onUpdate`, but no caller wires it. Phase 2 treats progress as opportunistic and non-blocking; a documented unsupported decision is acceptable.

## Scope
In scope: inspect Pi ExtensionAPI/tool execution context for progress/event/log support; if supported, wire line-buffered updates from `registerSubAgent()` to a safe host progress surface or structured debug logs; add practical callback tests with fake spawn output if plumbing exists; if unsupported, document that user-visible streaming is unsupported and retain structured logging only. Out of scope: background task lifecycle, mid-run steering, streaming nested stdout into final tool result, or noisy parent-context output.

## Components Touched
- `src/sub-agents/register.ts`
- `src/sub-agents/runner.ts` if callback semantics need adjustment
- logging/status docs as applicable
- callback/progress tests if supported

## Dependencies / Prerequisites
Can run after failure diagnostics; independent of fallback.

## Important Assumptions and Constraints
- Do not dump streaming output into final context or final tool result by default.
- Keep progress optional and noisy-output safe.
- If no safe host surface exists, document unsupported behavior instead of inventing one.

## Risks / Gotchas
Streaming raw nested output can leak secrets or overwhelm parent context; keep redaction/bounds aligned with failure-detail policy.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Either tests prove callback plumbing reaches a safe host progress/log surface, or docs clearly state user-visible streaming is unsupported.
- Final delegate tool result remains concise and does not include live stream logs by default.

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [POLISH] Reuse the Phase 1 redaction/truncation policy for any progress/log path. Progress callbacks should be line-buffered, bounded, and non-authoritative; final DelegateResult content should remain derived from completion/failure formatting, not from accumulated live progress lines. If only logger output is available, document the exact log level/category users can inspect.

title: Document and verify Phase 2 delegation quality
description:
## Objective
Stabilize Phase 2 improvements with updated docs, full verification, and explicit deferrals for future orchestration work.

## Context
After timeout, prompt, fallback, and progress decisions land, users need accurate configuration examples and known limitations before any Phase 3 planning.

## Scope
In scope: update README/config docs/YAML examples for timeout, promptMode, fallbackModels/fallback_models, progress support or unsupported decision, and read-only fallback policy; run `bun run lint`, `bun run build`, and `bun run test`; optionally run `bun run check:size`; capture deferred Phase 3 items including parallel fanout, background lifecycle, and worktree isolation. Out of scope: implementing Phase 3 features.

## Components Touched
- README/config docs/YAML examples
- optional AGENTS.md updates if command or convention docs changed
- release/roadmap notes if present

## Dependencies / Prerequisites
Depends on Phase 2 timeout, prompt builder, selective append decision, fallback, and progress spike beads.

## Important Assumptions and Constraints
- Docs must match implemented behavior exactly; do not promise append/fallback/progress where validation deferred it.
- Verification order remains `bun run lint`, `bun run build`, `bun run test`.

## Risks / Gotchas
Config examples should preserve backward compatibility: `model` remains a string; fallback is additive.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Docs and examples cover all implemented Phase 2 fields and limitations.
- Full verification passes.
- Deferred Phase 3 items are explicitly listed without implementing them.
